You said:
do you know the elecytoral commission of zambia

ChatGPT said:

Yes — I know about the Electoral Commission of Zambia (ECZ). I can give you an overview: what it is, what it does, and recent developments. If you want something specific (laws, upcoming elections, criticisms, etc.), I can pull that up too.

What is the ECZ?

  • The ECZ is the independent and autonomous electoral management body in Zambia, established under Article 229 of the Zambian Constitution. Electoral Commission of Zambia - Home+2parliament.gov.zm+2

  • It’s governed by the Electoral Commission Act, No. 25 of 2016 (with amendments like the 2019 amendment) which lays out its powers, membership, functions, etc. parliament.gov.zm+2parliament.gov.zm+2

Key Functions of the ECZ

According to law and official sources, the ECZ is required to:

  1. Register voters (continuous registration, mass registration) Electoral Commission of Zambia - Home+4Electoral Commission of Zambia - Home+4Zambia Monitor | Zambia Monitor+4

  2. Maintain the voters’ registers (both national and local authority) Electoral Commission of Zambia - Home+1

  3. Conduct and supervise elections — Presidential, National Assembly, Local Government (councillors, council chairpersons) Electoral Commission of Zambia - Home+3Electoral Commission of Zambia - Home+3Electoral Commission of Zambia - Home+3

  4. Delimit electoral boundaries — mapping constituencies, wards, polling districts appropriately. Electoral Commission of Zambia - Home+3Electoral Commission of Zambia - Home+3parliament.gov.zm+3

  5. Voter education / public awareness — ensure the electorate is informed about election dates, processes, rights, etc. Electoral Commission of Zambia - Home+2Electoral Commission of Zambia - Home+2

  6. Adjudicate minor electoral/ administrative disputes related to elections. Electoral Commission of Zambia - Home+2Electoral Commission of Zambia - Home+2

  7. Regulation of conduct of voters and candidates, accreditation of observers, etc. parliament.gov.zm+2Electoral Commission of Zambia - Home+2

Recent / Current Things

  • The ECZ plans a nationwide mass voter registration exercise beginning 13 October 2025, targeting about 3.5 million eligible voters. This is ahead of the 2026 general elections. Zambia Monitor | Zambia Monitor

  • They have also launched an online pre-registration platform to allow eligible citizens to begin the registration process online before completing it in-person at designated centres. Zambia Monitor | Zambia Monitor

  • The register of voters is to be certified by 30 April 2026, which is a key milestone in the elections roadmap. Zambia Monitor | Zambia Monitor+1

If you like, I can share the legal framework (actual code / statutes) governing the ECZ, or show you how its organizational structure is set up. Do you want me to pull that together (in code-like format, since you're a developer)?


You said:
Create hero image prompts and text

ChatGPT said:

Got it 👍
Let me give you hero image prompt ideas + matching text that you could use for the Electoral Commission of Zambia (ECZ) — for web banners, campaigns, or digital ads.

🎨 Hero Image Prompts (for AI image generation)

  1. National Unity & Voting

    • Prompt: "A diverse group of Zambians — young, old, rural, and urban — proudly holding up their voter’s cards in front of a ballot box, with the Zambian flag waving in the background, bright hopeful sunrise, professional clean banner style."

  2. Digital Transformation

    • Prompt: "Modern technology interface with a smartphone showing an ECZ voter registration app, futuristic glowing UI, Zambian flag colors flowing in the background, symbolizing secure online registration."

  3. Election Integrity

    • Prompt: "A transparent ballot box filled with ballots, overseen by officials in ECZ attire, glowing light rays symbolizing transparency, trust, and democracy, with Lusaka skyline faintly in the background."

  4. Youth Participation

    • Prompt: "Smiling young Zambians at a polling station, wearing colorful clothes, holding voter cards, cheerful atmosphere with 'Your Voice, Your Vote' text space, professional campaign style."

  5. Countdown to Elections

    • Prompt: "Large digital countdown clock in the middle of Lusaka city, symbolizing the upcoming 2026 elections, crowds of citizens gathering with flags and voter cards, inspirational tone."

📝 Matching Hero Text (headline + tagline)

  1. Headline: “Your Voice. Your Power. Your Vote.”
    Tagline: Be part of Zambia’s future — register today with the Electoral Commission of Zambia.

  2. Headline: “Every Vote Counts.”
    Tagline: Together we strengthen Zambia’s democracy.

  3. Headline: “Smart, Secure, Simple.”
    Tagline: Register online with ECZ and be ready for the 2026 elections.

  4. Headline: “The Future is in Your Hands.”
    Tagline: Zambia decides — make sure your voice is heard.

  5. Headline: “Trusted Elections. Stronger Democracy.”
    Tagline: The ECZ — ensuring free, fair, and credible elections.

👉 Do you want me to design these ready-to-use in HTML/CSS hero section code (with background + overlay + call-to-action button), or just keep them as creative text + prompts?


You said:
Create prompts that sre creative and realistic. we want to bring the nations human element to life
3/3

ChatGPT said:

Perfect! You want hero image prompts that capture real human moments in Zambia, highlighting diversity, emotion, and civic participation — not just staged “stock” images. Here’s a set of creative, realistic prompts for AI generation:

🎨 Human-Centered Hero Image Prompts

  1. Community Voter Registration

    • Prompt: “A small rural Zambian community gathered outside a school converted into a registration center, children playing in the background, adults chatting while receiving voter cards, natural sunlight, warm and authentic, documentary-style photography, capturing real human interaction.”

  2. Urban Youth Voting

    • Prompt: “Young Zambian adults lining up at a bustling Lusaka polling station, laughing and talking, holding voter cards, colorful clothing, sunlight streaming through windows, candid and dynamic composition, human-focused realism.”

  3. Family Civic Moment

    • Prompt: “A multi-generational Zambian family — grandparents, parents, and teenagers — walking together to cast their votes, natural expressions, casual clothing, morning light, capturing pride and community spirit, cinematic documentary style.”

  4. Election Day Energy

    • Prompt: “A crowded polling station in a small town, volunteers helping elderly voters, people smiling, hands exchanging voter cards, posters in local languages on the walls, natural lighting, high-detail, human connection and energy emphasized.”

  5. Inclusive Democracy

    • Prompt: “A visually impaired Zambian woman casting her vote with the help of an election officer, surrounded by friendly observers, genuine expressions, soft afternoon light, realistic textures, emphasizing accessibility and human dignity.”

  6. Rural Celebration of Voting

    • Prompt: “Villagers walking along a dusty path to a voting center, carrying voter cards and talking with neighbors, vibrant natural colors, authentic rural environment, candid style, emphasizing community participation and togetherness.”

📝 Human-Focused Hero Text

  1. Headline: “Zambia Votes. Zambia Voices.”
    Tagline: Every citizen, every story, every vote counts.

  2. Headline: “From Every Corner, We Come Together.”
    Tagline: Strengthening democracy through real people, real communities.

  3. Headline: “Your Moment to Be Counted.”
    Tagline: Walk in, speak out, and shape Zambia’s future.

  4. Headline: “Democracy in Action.”
    Tagline: Celebrating the people who make Zambia’s elections real.

  5. Headline: “Every Hand, Every Voice, Every Vote.”
    Tagline: Because democracy is about people, not just politics.

If you want, I can refine these further into 10–15 ultra-realistic hero prompts that capture specific Zambian cultural cues, attire, and landscapes, ready for AI generation with cinematic, documentary-style realism.

Do you want me to do that next?

Nice — focusing on one young person makes the message powerful and relatable. Here are creative, hyper-realistic, human-centered prompts built around single youth subjects, grounded in real Zambian environments and emotions.

🎨 Single Youth – Realistic Human Hero Prompts

1. First-Time Voter Pride

Prompt:
"Portrait of a young Zambian man standing outside a polling station, holding his voter card close to his chest, natural confident smile, early morning golden sunlight, shallow depth of field, real documentary photography style, authentic street background with subtle Zambian flag colors visible."

2. Quiet Determination

Prompt:
"Close-up portrait of a young Zambian woman sitting on public transport heading to a voting center, looking out the window thoughtfully while holding a voter card, natural lighting, realistic skin texture, candid emotional storytelling, cinematic realism."

3. Rural Youth Representation

Prompt:
"Young Zambian farmer in early 20s standing in a maize field at sunrise, holding voter card in one hand, dusty boots and work clothes, authentic rural Zambia landscape, warm natural light, highly realistic documentary photography."

4. University Student Civic Moment

Prompt:
"Young Zambian university student standing outside campus gates, backpack over one shoulder, voter card visible in hand, modern African campus environment, natural candid pose, soft afternoon light, realistic lifestyle photography."

5. Youth Leadership Energy

Prompt:
"Confident young Zambian woman standing in a busy Lusaka street market, looking directly into camera, holding voter card casually, people moving blurred in background, strong natural lighting, editorial documentary style realism."

6. Hopeful Future Builder

Prompt:
"Young Zambian man sitting on concrete steps in a township neighborhood, tying shoelaces while voter card rests beside him, raw authentic moment, overcast natural light, cinematic street photography realism."

7. Early Morning Commitment

Prompt:
"Young Zambian woman walking alone down a quiet road at sunrise toward a polling station, long natural shadows, holding voter card in hand, emotional storytelling, ultra realistic documentary photo style."

8. Tech-Savvy Young Voter

Prompt:
"Young Zambian youth standing outside a modern building checking voter registration confirmation on smartphone, voter card partially visible in other hand, realistic urban Zambia setting, natural daylight, authentic candid realism."

9. Reflective Youth Portrait

Prompt:
"Studio-style natural portrait of a young Zambian voter holding voter card near face, neutral background, soft natural window light, ultra detailed skin texture, emotional eye contact, high-end documentary realism."

10. After Voting Moment

Prompt:
"Young Zambian first-time voter walking away from polling station smiling quietly to themselves, holding voter card, subtle ink mark on finger, real outdoor lighting, cinematic documentary photo realism."

You said:
 i want to use it for the hero section that already has a background

ChatGPT said:

Ah — so you need subject-focused prompts that will sit on top of an existing hero background.
That means we should aim for:

  • Clean composition

  • Subject slightly off-center (for text space)

  • Neutral or soft edges (so it blends into background)

  • Real human emotion (not campaign-poster stiff)

  • Lighting that works with overlays (rim light, soft light, studio natural)

🎨 Youth Subject Prompts (Foreground / Overlay Friendly)

1. Clean Portrait – Left or Right Placement

Prompt:
"Ultra realistic portrait of a young Zambian adult standing facing slightly toward camera, relaxed natural expression, neutral clothing in soft earthy tones, soft studio daylight lighting, clean edges around subject, shallow depth of field, isolated human subject suitable for website hero overlay, documentary photography realism."

2. Confident Standing Youth (Hero Banner Style)

Prompt:
"Young Zambian youth standing confidently with relaxed posture, arms naturally at sides, subtle proud expression, natural skin texture, soft directional light from one side, minimal shadow spill, clean subject separation, realistic editorial photography, designed for website hero foreground placement."

3. Youth Holding Voter Card (Subtle, Not Forced)

Prompt:
"Realistic portrait of young Zambian first-time voter holding voter card naturally at chest level, not posed, candid expression, soft natural window lighting, neutral background edges, high detail skin realism, documentary photography style, subject ready for transparent or blended hero section placement."

4. Looking Toward Text Space (Great for Hero Layouts)

Prompt:
"Young Zambian woman standing and looking slightly off frame as if toward website headline text, calm hopeful expression, soft rim lighting, realistic skin and hair detail, neutral wardrobe, shallow depth of field, clean subject edges, ultra realistic lifestyle photography."

5. Walking Forward (Dynamic but Clean)

Prompt:
"Young Zambian man mid-step walking slowly forward, natural movement, confident relaxed face, soft natural daylight, slight motion realism but sharp focus on subject, minimal environmental distractions, cinematic documentary realism, designed for hero section foreground subject."

6. Close-Up Emotional Connection

Prompt:
"Close up portrait of young Zambian youth with natural eye contact, soft light across face, ultra realistic skin detail, natural hair texture, neutral tones, shallow depth of field, clean edges, premium documentary photography style for website hero overlay."

7. Soft Smile – Trust & Approachability

Prompt:
"Young Zambian adult with gentle natural smile, relaxed shoulders, casual modern clothing, soft daylight lighting, realistic texture detail, clean subject isolation, realistic editorial photography, suitable for website hero foreground subject."

8. Modern Civic Youth

Prompt:
"Young Zambian urban youth standing casually, modern but realistic clothing, natural confident posture, soft cinematic lighting, shallow depth of field, minimal background distraction, ultra realistic human portrait for hero section composition."

🧠 Pro Tip (For Your Existing Hero Background)

If your hero already has strong colors or scenery, add this to prompts:

  • "neutral color harmony"

  • "soft edge lighting"

  • "no busy background"

  • "subject isolated"

  • "foreground subject for web banner composition"

If you want, I can go even more production-level, like:

👉 Prompts optimized specifically for Midjourney v6 / SDXL / DALL-E style
👉 Prompts tailored for dark hero background vs light hero background
👉 Prompts designed around ECZ brand tone (trust, unity, youth empowerment)

Which direction are you leaning?

You said:
i want to add an image in the background: .tp-header-4-top { background: var(--tp-theme-primary); border-bottom: none; }

ChatGPT said:

You can add a background image while keeping your theme color as an overlay using layered backgrounds.

Here are clean production-ready options depending on what effect you want.

✅ Option 1 — Image + Color Overlay (Recommended for Hero Headers)

CSS
.tp-header-4-top {
  background:
    linear-gradient(rgba(13, 145, 83, 0.85), rgba(13, 145, 83, 0.85)),
    url("../img/header/header-bg.jpg");

  background-size: cover;
  background-position: center;
  background-repeat: no-repeat;
  border-bottom: none;
}

👉 Keeps brand color
👉 Makes text readable
👉 Works well for headers

✅ Option 2 — Image Only (No Color Overlay)

CSS
.tp-header-4-top {
  background-image: url("../img/header/header-bg.jpg");
  background-size: cover;
  background-position: center;
  background-repeat: no-repeat;
  border-bottom: none;
}

✅ Option 3 — Theme Color Fallback + Image

CSS
.tp-header-4-top {
  background-color: var(--tp-theme-primary);
  background-image: url("../img/header/header-bg.jpg");
  background-size: cover;
  background-position: center;
  background-repeat: no-repeat;
  border-bottom: none;
}

✅ Option 4 — Darken Image Only (No Brand Color Tint)

CSS
.tp-header-4-top {
  background:
    linear-gradient(rgba(0,0,0,0.4), rgba(0,0,0,0.4)),
    url("../img/header/header-bg.jpg");

  background-size: cover;
  background-position: center;
  background-repeat: no-repeat;
  border-bottom: none;
}

⭐ Pro Production Tip

If text sits on top:

CSS
background-attachment: fixed; /* optional parallax feel */

If you want, I can tailor this exactly for your template — for example:

  • TP theme header transparency behavior

  • Sticky header version

  • Mobile optimized background loading

  • Laravel Blade asset path version ({{ asset('...') }})

Just tell me 👍


You said:
suggest the image resolutiuon

ChatGPT said:

For a header / hero background image, you want something large enough for wide monitors but optimized for web performance.

✅ Recommended Hero / Header Background Resolution

⭐ Best Single Size (Safe Default)

1920 × 1080 (Full HD)
👉 Works on most laptops + desktops
👉 Good balance of quality + file size
👉 Standard for web hero sections

⭐ If You Want Premium / Future Proof

2560 × 1440 (2K)
👉 Sharper on large monitors
👉 Still manageable for web optimization

⭐ Ultra High-End (Only if Optimized Properly)

3840 × 2160 (4K)
👉 Only if you serve compressed / responsive images
👉 Otherwise file size will hurt load time

📱 Mobile Consideration (If Using Same Image)

Minimum safe crop area inside:
1200 × 800 center safe zone

Because mobile will crop edges.

🎯 Real-World Web Dev Recommendation (For You)

Since this is a header strip (not full screen hero):

👉 Export at 1920 × 600 – 1920 × 800

If full hero:
👉 Export at 1920 × 1080

⚡ File Optimization Targets

ResolutionIdeal File Size
1920 wide200KB – 400KB
2560 wide300KB – 600KB

Use:

  • WebP (best)

  • Optimized JPG (fallback)

⭐ Pro Tip (Most Devs Miss This)

Design at 2× size, export at web size.

Example:
Design: 3840 × 2160
Export: 1920 × 1080

You get sharper detail.

If you want, tell me:

👉 Full hero section OR thin header strip
👉 Dark overlay OR bright image
👉 People-focused OR environment-focused

…and I’ll give you the exact pixel spec + safe text zones.

make the color darker: .tp-header-4-top { background: linear-gradient(rgba(13, 145, 83, 0.85), rgba(13, 145, 83, 0.85)), url("../img/header/header-bg.jpg"); background-size: cover; background-position: center; background-repeat: no-repeat; border-bottom: none; }

ChatGPT said:

To make it darker, you mainly increase the overlay strength or use a darker green tone.

Here are clean options.

✅ Option 1 — Increase Overlay Opacity (Simplest)

CSS
.tp-header-4-top {
  background:
    linear-gradient(rgba(13, 145, 83, 0.95), rgba(13, 145, 83, 0.95)),
    url("../img/header/header-bg.jpg");

  background-size: cover;
  background-position: center;
  background-repeat: no-repeat;
  border-bottom: none;
}

👉 Same color
👉 Much darker appearance

✅ Option 2 — Darker Green + Strong Overlay (Better Visual Depth)

CSS
.tp-header-4-top {
  background:
    linear-gradient(rgba(7, 90, 50, 0.92), rgba(7, 90, 50, 0.92)),
    url("../img/header/header-bg.jpg");

  background-size: cover;
  background-position: center;
  background-repeat: no-repeat;
  border-bottom: none;
}

👉 Richer, more premium green
👉 Better contrast for white text

✅ Option 3 — Brand Green + Dark Blend (Most Cinematic)

CSS
.tp-header-4-top {
  background:
    linear-gradient(rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.45), rgba(13, 145, 83, 0.90)),
    url("../img/header/header-bg.jpg");

  background-size: cover;
  background-position: center;
  background-repeat: no-repeat;
  border-bottom: none;
}

👉 Dark top → branded bottom
👉 Looks very modern

⭐ If This Is For Navigation Readability

Use Option 2 — safest for UI clarity.

If this is hero visual impact
Use Option 3 — most design-heavy.

Her hair is straight and black, reaching to her shoulders. Her facial expression appears contemplative, with a slight furrow of the brow and her lips slightly parted. ChatGPT said:

Here’s a version tailored for an ECZ website hero section — realistic, human, Zambian context, civic-focused, and composition-friendly for hero text overlay.

🎨 ECZ Hero Section Prompt (Refined)

Prompt:
"A young Zambian woman in her early twenties is the main subject, positioned slightly to the right of center, facing toward the upper right in a natural three-quarter view. She is wearing a smart casual outfit — a dark green or neutral blazer over a light top — conveying professionalism and civic responsibility. Her natural hair is neatly styled to shoulder length. Her expression is thoughtful and hopeful, with calm confident eyes and relaxed natural posture.

The background shows a realistic Zambian civic environment — a modern public service building or voter registration center with subtle glass panels and signage, softly blurred people moving naturally in the background to show real public activity. Lighting is natural daylight with soft warm African sunlight tones, creating an atmosphere of trust, dignity, and national pride.

Shot from medium distance with sharp focus on the subject and gentle depth of field blur in the background. Composition leaves clean negative space on the left side for website hero text. Ultra realistic documentary photography style, natural skin texture, authentic African color grading, cinematic but realistic lighting."

Add this line at the end:

"Subtle Zambia flag color harmony in overall color palette, realistic civic documentary photography style."

You said:
add remiox icons: <span class="btn-icon"><i class="fa-sharp fa-regular fa-arrow-right"></i></span>

ChatGPT said:

To switch from Font Awesome to Remix Icon, replace the <i> class with the Remix equivalent.

✅ Remix Icon Version (Arrow Right)

HTML
<span class="btn-icon">
  <i class="ri-arrow-right-line"></i>
</span>

✅ If You Want a Filled Arrow

HTML
<span class="btn-icon">
  <i class="ri-arrow-right-fill"></i>
</span>

✅ If Remix Icon Is Not Loaded Yet

Add in your <head>:

HTML
<link href="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/remixicon@4.2.0/fonts/remixicon.css" rel="stylesheet">

⭐ Good ECZ Button Icon Alternatives

👉 Clean / Standard

Code
ri-arrow-right-line

👉 Bold / CTA Feel

Code
ri-arrow-right-circle-line

👉 More Modern Motion Feel

Code
ri-arrow-right-up-line

If you want, I can suggest icon sets for the whole ECZ site (navigation, voters, security, registration, results, etc.) so everything stays consistent.

Here’s an ECZ-adapted version of your section — keeping your structure, classes, animations, and layout intact, but changing:

✅ Content → ECZ civic services
✅ Icons → Remix Icons (clean + modern)
✅ CTAs → Voter / public service focused
✅ Form → Voter information / updates instead of careers
✅ Tone → Public trust, participation, democracy

✅ ECZ Adapted Section (Drop-In Ready)

HTML
<div class="tp-join-area pt-20">
    <div class="container-fluid container-1690">
        <div class="row gx-20">

            <!-- Register as a Voter -->
            <div class="col-lg-4 col-md-6 mb-30">
                <div class="tp-join-4-item tp-bg-common-white tp-round-4 h-100 wow fadeInLeft" data-wow-delay=".3s" data-wow-duration=".9s">
                    
                    <div class="tp-join-4-icon d-flex justify-content-between align-items-center mb-30">
                        <span class="icon tp-bg-grey-3 d-inline-block text-center tp-round-4">
                            <i class="ri-id-card-line fs-28"></i>
                        </span>

                        <a class="icon-2 d-inline-block rounded-circle text-center" href="#">
                            <i class="ri-arrow-right-line"></i>
                        </a>
                    </div>

                    <div class="tp-join-4-content">
                        <h4 class="ls-m-3 fs-24 mb-15">Register as a Voter</h4>
                        <p class="tp-text-grey-2">
                            Confirm your eligibility and register to vote. Ensure your details are correct 
                            to participate in upcoming national and local elections.
                        </p>
                    </div>
                </div>
            </div>

            <!-- Verify Voter Information -->
            <div class="col-lg-4 col-md-6 mb-30">
                <div class="tp-join-4-item tp-bg-grey-3 tp-round-4 h-100 wow fadeInUp" data-wow-delay=".3s" data-wow-duration=".9s">
                    
                    <div class="tp-join-4-icon d-flex justify-content-between align-items-center mb-30">
                        <span class="icon tp-bg-common-white d-inline-block text-center tp-round-4">
                            <i class="ri-search-eye-line fs-28"></i>
                        </span>

                        <a class="icon-2 d-inline-block rounded-circle text-center" href="#">
                            <i class="ri-arrow-right-line"></i>
                        </a>
                    </div>

                    <div class="tp-join-4-content">
                        <h4 class="ls-m-3 fs-24 mb-15">Verify Voter Details</h4>
                        <p class="tp-text-grey-2">
                            Check your voter registration status, polling station location, 
                            and ensure your information is up to date in the voters register.
                        </p>
                    </div>
                </div>
            </div>

            <!-- Voter Updates / Notifications -->
            <div class="col-lg-4 mb-30">
                <div class="tp-join-form-wrap tp-join-4-form tp-bg-common-white tp-round-4 h-100 wow fadeInRight" data-wow-delay=".3s" data-wow-duration=".9s">
                    
                    <h3 class="mb-25 fs-24">Get Election Updates</h3>

                    <form action="#">
                        <input class="tp-input mb-10" type="text" placeholder="Email address*">
                        <input class="tp-input mb-10" type="text" placeholder="Province or District">

                        <select class="select tp-input mb-10" name="service">
                            <option value="">Select Update Type</option>
                            <option value="registration">Voter Registration Updates</option>
                            <option value="polling">Polling Station Information</option>
                            <option value="election">Election Announcements</option>
                            <option value="education">Voter Education Notices</option>
                        </select>

                        <button class="tp-btn-xs tp-round-4 fs-16 tp-bg-theme-primary w-100 d-block tp-text-common-white hover-text-white fw-500" type="submit">
                            Subscribe for Updates
                        </button>
                    </form>
                </div>
            </div>

        </div>
    </div>
</div>

✅ Remix Icons Used

Make sure this is loaded:

HTML
<link href="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/remixicon@4.2.0/fonts/remixicon.css" rel="stylesheet">

  • Actionable civic services (Register, Verify, Stay Informed)

  • Youth + digital + accessibility friendly

  • Matches government trust style

ChatGPT said:

Below is updated code using Remix Icons and real ECZ contact information based on verified public records.

✅ Real ECZ Details Used

  • Organisation: Electoral Commission of Zambia (ECZ)

  • Address: Haile Selassie Avenue, Lusaka, Zambia

  • Postal Code: 10101

  • Phone: +260 211 253155

  • Email: elections@elections.org.zm

  • Website: www.elections.org.zm

Source confirms ECZ HQ is on Haile Selassie Avenue in Lusaka with the listed contact details. zambia-v3-staging.eurodyn.com

✅ Updated Code (Remix Icons + Real ECZ Info)

HTML
<div class="col-lg-9">
  <div class="tp-header-3-location">
    <ul>

      <!-- ADDRESS -->
      <li>
        <span class="icons">
          <i class="ri-map-pin-2-fill" style="color:#0d9153; font-size:18px;"></i>
        </span>
        <p class="mb-0 lh-24">
          <a href="https://www.google.com/maps/search/Haile+Selassie+Avenue+Lusaka+Zambia" target="_blank">
            Electoral Commission of Zambia<br>
            Haile Selassie Avenue, Lusaka 10101, Zambia
          </a>
        </p>
      </li>

      <!-- EMAIL -->
      <li>
        <span class="icons">
          <i class="ri-mail-fill" style="color:#0d9153; font-size:18px;"></i>
        </span>
        <p class="mb-0 lh-24">
          Email:<br>
          <a href="mailto:elections@elections.org.zm">
            elections@elections.org.zm
          </a>
        </p>
      </li>

      <!-- PHONE -->
      <li>
        <span class="icons">
          <i class="ri-phone-fill" style="color:#0d9153; font-size:18px;"></i>
        </span>
        <p class="mb-0 lh-24">
          Call Us:<br>
          <a href="tel:+260211253155">
            +260 211 253155
          </a>
        </p>
      </li>

      <!-- WEBSITE -->
      <li>
        <span class="icons">
          <i class="ri-global-fill" style="color:#0d9153; font-size:18px;"></i>
        </span>
        <p class="mb-0 lh-24">
          Official Website:<br>
          <a href="https://www.elections.org.zm" target="_blank">
            www.elections.org.zm
          </a>
        </p>
      </li>

    </ul>
  </div>
</div>

✅ Notes for Your ECZ UI

  • Using ri-map-pin-2-fill, ri-mail-fill, ri-phone-fill, ri-global-fill keeps it consistent with government-style UI.

  • Color uses ECZ green tone.

  • Clickable tel + mail improves accessibility.

If you want, I can:

  • Convert this into a Blade component

  • Make it responsive for mobile header strip

  • Add top-bar micro layout (like govt portal style)

  • Or match it exactly to your tp-header theme spacing system

Just tell me.

ChatGPT said:

Here is your offcanvas updated to Remix Icons + ECZ-friendly contact + cleaned social icons while keeping your structure intact.

I removed inline SVG social icons and replaced them with Remix for consistency and maintainability.

✅ Updated Offcanvas (Remix Icons + ECZ Ready)

HTML
<div class="tpoffcanvas-area">
  <div class="tpoffcanvas">

    <!-- CLOSE BUTTON -->
    <div class="tpoffcanvas__close-btn">
      <button class="close-btn">
        <i class="ri-close-line"></i>
      </button>
    </div>

    <!-- LOGO -->
    <div class="tpoffcanvas__logo">
      <a href="index.html">
        <img data-width="140" src="assets/img/logo/logo-black.png" alt="ECZ Logo">
      </a>
    </div>

    <!-- MOBILE MENU -->
    <div class="tp-offcanvas-menu d-xl-none">
      <nav></nav>
    </div>

    <!-- CTA BUTTON -->
    <div class="tpoffcanvas-btn-1 mb-50">
      <a class="tpoffcanvas-btn" href="contact.html">
        Contact ECZ
        <i class="ri-arrow-right-line"></i>
      </a>
    </div>

    <!-- CONTACT INFO -->
    <div class="tpoffcanvas__contact-info">
      <div class="tpoffcanvas__contact-title">
        <h5>Contact Electoral Commission of Zambia</h5>
      </div>

      <ul>

        <!-- ADDRESS -->
        <li>
          <span>
            <i class="ri-map-pin-2-fill" style="color:#0d9153;"></i>
          </span>
          <a href="https://www.google.com/maps/search/Haile+Selassie+Avenue+Lusaka+Zambia" target="_blank">
            Haile Selassie Avenue, Lusaka, Zambia
          </a>
        </li>

        <!-- EMAIL -->
        <li>
          <span>
            <i class="ri-mail-fill" style="color:#0d9153;"></i>
          </span>
          <a href="mailto:elections@elections.org.zm">
            elections@elections.org.zm
          </a>
        </li>

        <!-- PHONE -->
        <li>
          <span>
            <i class="ri-phone-fill" style="color:#0d9153;"></i>
          </span>
          <a href="tel:+260211253155">
            +260 211 253155
          </a>
        </li>

      </ul>
    </div>

    <!-- SOCIAL -->
    <div class="tpoffcanvas__social">
      <div class="social-icon">

        <a href="#" aria-label="Facebook">
          <i class="ri-facebook-fill"></i>
        </a>

        <a href="#" aria-label="Twitter / X">
          <i class="ri-twitter-x-fill"></i>
        </a>

        <a href="#" aria-label="Instagram">
          <i class="ri-instagram-line"></i>
        </a>

        <a href="#" aria-label="YouTube">
          <i class="ri-youtube-fill"></i>
        </a>

      </div>
    </div>

  </div>
</div>

✅ Make Sure Remix Is Loaded

HTML
<link href="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/remixicon@4.2.0/fonts/remixicon.css" rel="stylesheet">

⭐ Why This Is Better for ECZ

✔ Government clean UI
✔ No heavy inline SVG clutter
✔ Easy icon swapping later
✔ Consistent brand color
✔ Accessibility improved (aria labels)

If you want next, I can:

  • Convert this into Laravel Blade component

  • Add ECZ brand hover effects

  • Make offcanvas glass + gradient ECZ style

  • Add dynamic menu injection for CMS / Laravel routes

Just tell me.


You said:
make the color of the placeholders black: <input class="tp-input mb-10" type="text" placeholder="Email address*"> <input class="tp-input mb-10" type="text" placeholder="Province or District">

ChatGPT said:

You can make the placeholder text black using CSS. Here’s the simplest way for your inputs:

HTML
<input class="tp-input mb-10" type="text" placeholder="Email address*">
<input class="tp-input mb-10" type="text" placeholder="Province or District">

<style>
/* Standard placeholder color */
.tp-input::placeholder {
    color: black;
    opacity: 1; /* Ensures full color in all browsers */
}

/* For IE 10-11 */
.tp-input:-ms-input-placeholder {
    color: black;
}

/* For Edge */
.tp-input::-ms-input-placeholder {
    color: black;
}
</style>

This will make the placeholder text appear black across modern browsers.

If you want, I can also update your offcanvas inputs to match ECZ black placeholders so it’s consistent. Do you want me to do that?


You said:
change this to something else: <div class="col-lg-4 col-md-6 mb-30"> <div class="tp-join-4-item tp-bg-common-white tp-round-4 h-100 wow fadeInLeft" data-wow-delay=".3s" data-wow-duration=".9s"> <div class="tp-join-4-icon d-flex justify-content-between align-items-center mb-30"> <span class="icon tp-bg-grey-3 d-inline-block text-center tp-round-4"> <i style="color:#f17121" class="ri-id-card-line fs-28"></i> </span> <a class="icon-2 d-inline-block rounded-circle text-center" href="#"> <i class="ri-arrow-right-line"></i> </a> </div> <div class="tp-join-4-content"> <h4 class="ls-m-3 fs-24 mb-15">Register as a Voter</h4> <p class="tp-text-grey-2"> Confirm your eligibility and register to vote. Ensure your details are correct to participate in upcoming national and local elections. </p> </div> </div> </div>

ChatGPT said:

Here’s a revised version with updated content and styling, keeping the same structure but changing the purpose and text to something more general for the Electoral Commission of Zambia context:

HTML
<div class="col-lg-4 col-md-6 mb-30">
    <div class="tp-join-4-item tp-bg-common-white tp-round-4 h-100 wow fadeInLeft" data-wow-delay=".3s" data-wow-duration=".9s">
        
        <div class="tp-join-4-icon d-flex justify-content-between align-items-center mb-30">
            <span class="icon tp-bg-grey-3 d-inline-block text-center tp-round-4">
                <i style="color:#f17121" class="ri-government-line fs-28"></i>
            </span>

            <a class="icon-2 d-inline-block rounded-circle text-center" href="https://www.elections.org.zm/" target="_blank">
                <i class="ri-arrow-right-line"></i>
            </a>
        </div>

        <div class="tp-join-4-content">
            <h4 class="ls-m-3 fs-24 mb-15">Check Your Voter Status</h4>
            <p class="tp-text-grey-2">
                Verify your registration status and ensure your information is up-to-date. 
                Stay ready to participate in all upcoming national and local elections.
            </p>
        </div>
    </div>
</div>

Changes made:

  • Icon changed from ri-id-card-line to ri-government-line to suit the voter status/check theme.

  • Heading changed to “Check Your Voter Status”.

  • Paragraph updated to reflect checking registration rather than registering.

  • The arrow link now points to the Electoral Commission of Zambia website (https://www.elections.org.zm/).

I can also create 3–4 more similar cards for other ECZ actions like “Update Your Details,” “Find Your Polling Station,” and “Election Results” so the section looks complete. Do you want me to do that next?


You said:
Blend this with an image background: .tp-bg-grey-3 { background-color: var(--tp-grey-3); }

ChatGPT said:

You can blend the icon background with an image while keeping the grey overlay using CSS background-blend-mode. Here’s an example:

CSS
.tp-bg-grey-3 {
    background-color: var(--tp-grey-3); /* existing grey */
    background-image: url('assets/img/backgrounds/icon-bg.jpg'); /* your image */
    background-size: cover;
    background-position: center;
    background-repeat: no-repeat;
    background-blend-mode: multiply; /* blends grey with the image */
    color: #fff; /* optional, for icon contrast */
}

Explanation:

  • background-image → sets the image behind the icon.

  • background-blend-mode: multiply → blends the grey with the image. You can try overlay or screen for different effects.

  • background-size: cover → ensures the image fully covers the element.

  • Keep the icon color visible with color: #fff if needed.

If you want, I can rewrite your whole card with this blended icon background so it looks ready for production. Do you want me to do that?


You said:
I cannot see the image: .tp-bg-grey-3 { background-color: var(--tp-grey-3); /* existing grey */ background-image: url('assets/img/bg.jpg'); /* your image */ background-size: cover; background-position: center; background-repeat: no-repeat; background-blend-mode: multiply; /* blends grey with the image */ }

ChatGPT said:

Ah — the issue is that background-blend-mode: multiply; multiplies the background color with the image, which can make the image almost invisible if the grey (var(--tp-grey-3)) is too dark.

You have a few options to make the image visible:

Option 1: Use overlay instead of multiply

CSS
.tp-bg-grey-3 {
    background-color: var(--tp-grey-3);
    background-image: url('assets/img/bg.jpg');
    background-size: cover;
    background-position: center;
    background-repeat: no-repeat;
    background-blend-mode: overlay; /* lighter, more visible image */
}

Option 2: Reduce the opacity of the color

CSS
.tp-bg-grey-3 {
    background-color: rgba(128,128,128,0.5); /* semi-transparent grey */
    background-image: url('assets/img/bg.jpg');
    background-size: cover;
    background-position: center;
    background-repeat: no-repeat;
    background-blend-mode: normal; /* no blending, grey is see-through */
}

Option 3: Put the image as a pseudo-element

This ensures the image is visible and the grey acts as a semi-transparent overlay:

CSS
.tp-bg-grey-3 {
    position: relative;
    z-index: 1;
}

.tp-bg-grey-3::before {
    content: '';
    position: absolute;
    top: 0; left: 0; right: 0; bottom: 0;
    background-image: url('assets/img/bg.jpg');
    background-size: cover;
    background-position: center;
    opacity: 0.6; /* adjust as needed */
    z-index: -1;
}

If you want, I can rewrite your .tp-join-4-item card so the icon background has the image clearly visible while keeping the overlay and icon style. This will be fully production-ready.

Do you want me to do that?

plans in response changing circumstances or demands</p> </div> </div> </div> </div> </div> These are the functions and use remix icons: Functions Delimitation Delimitation is the process of mapping and re-aligning of electoral boundaries. Voter Registration The Electoral Act empowers the commission to carry out continuous voters registration. Voter Education The Electoral Process Act No. 35 of 2016 Section 80 states that the Commission shall provide Voter Education for an election. Elections Formal process by which registered voters cast their ballot to choose an individual to hold office of the President, Mayor, Council Chairperson, Member of National Assembly or Councillor. Conflict Management One of the mandates of the Electoral Commission of Zambia (the Commission) is to manage electoral conflicts and resolve disputes. This mandate is set out in section 113 of the Electoral Process Act No.35 2016. Accreditation Elections in Zambia are open to observation and monitoring by international and local organisations respectively.

ChatGPT said:
HTML
<div class="tp-feature-area tp-bg-grey-3 pt-100 pb-60">
    <div class="container">
        <div class="row">

            <!-- Delimitation -->
            <div class="col-lg-4 col-md-6">
                <div class="tp-feature-item tpshake-wrap mb-30 wow fadeInLeft" data-wow-delay=".2s" data-wow-duration=".9s">
                    <div class="tp-feature-title d-flex align-items-center mb-20">
                        <span class="tp-feature-icon tpshake mr-30">
                            <i class="ri-map-2-line" style="font-size:60px;"></i>
                        </span>
                        <h4 class="ls-m-2 lh-32">
                            Delimitation
                        </h4>
                    </div>
                    <p class="tp-text-grey-2">
                        Delimitation is the process of mapping and re-aligning of electoral boundaries.
                    </p>
                </div>
            </div>

            <!-- Voter Registration -->
            <div class="col-lg-4 col-md-6">
                <div class="tp-feature-item tpshake-wrap mb-30 wow fadeInLeft" data-wow-delay=".3s" data-wow-duration=".9s">
                    <div class="tp-feature-title d-flex align-items-center mb-20">
                        <span class="tp-feature-icon tpshake mr-30">
                            <i class="ri-user-add-line" style="font-size:60px;"></i>
                        </span>
                        <h4 class="ls-m-2 lh-32">
                            Voter Registration
                        </h4>
                    </div>
                    <p class="tp-text-grey-2">
                        The Electoral Act empowers the commission to carry out continuous voters registration.
                    </p>
                </div>
            </div>

            <!-- Voter Education -->
            <div class="col-lg-4 col-md-6">
                <div class="tp-feature-item tpshake-wrap mb-30 wow fadeInLeft" data-wow-delay=".4s" data-wow-duration=".9s">
                    <div class="tp-feature-title d-flex align-items-center mb-20">
                        <span class="tp-feature-icon tpshake mr-30">
                            <i class="ri-book-open-line" style="font-size:60px;"></i>
                        </span>
                        <h4 class="ls-m-2 lh-32">
                            Voter Education
                        </h4>
                    </div>
                    <p class="tp-text-grey-2">
                        The Electoral Process Act No. 35 of 2016 Section 80 states that the Commission shall provide Voter Education for an election.
                    </p>
                </div>
            </div>

            <!-- Elections -->
            <div class="col-lg-4 col-md-6">
                <div class="tp-feature-item tpshake-wrap mb-30 wow fadeInLeft" data-wow-delay=".5s" data-wow-duration=".9s">
                    <div class="tp-feature-title d-flex align-items-center mb-20">
                        <span class="tp-feature-icon tpshake mr-30">
                            <i class="ri-checkbox-circle-line" style="font-size:60px;"></i>
                        </span>
                        <h4 class="ls-m-2 lh-32">
                            Elections
                        </h4>
                    </div>
                    <p class="tp-text-grey-2">
                        Formal process by which registered voters cast their ballot to choose leaders such as the President, Mayor, Council Chairperson, Members of National Assembly or Councillors.
                    </p>
                </div>
            </div>

            <!-- Conflict Management -->
            <div class="col-lg-4 col-md-6">
                <div class="tp-feature-item tpshake-wrap mb-30 wow fadeInLeft" data-wow-delay=".6s" data-wow-duration=".9s">
                    <div class="tp-feature-title d-flex align-items-center mb-20">
                        <span class="tp-feature-icon tpshake mr-30">
                            <i class="ri-shield-check-line" style="font-size:60px;"></i>
                        </span>
                        <h4 class="ls-m-2 lh-32">
                            Conflict Management
                        </h4>
                    </div>
                    <p class="tp-text-grey-2">
                        The Commission manages electoral conflicts and resolves disputes in line with Section 113 of the Electoral Process Act No. 35 of 2016.
                    </p>
                </div>
            </div>

            <!-- Accreditation -->
            <div class="col-lg-4 col-md-6">
                <div class="tp-feature-item tpshake-wrap mb-30 wow fadeInLeft" data-wow-delay=".7s" data-wow-duration=".9s">
                    <div class="tp-feature-title d-flex align-items-center mb-20">
                        <span class="tp-feature-icon tpshake mr-30">
                            <i class="ri-team-line" style="font-size:60px;"></i>
                        </span>
                        <h4 class="ls-m-2 lh-32">
                            Accreditation
                        </h4>
                    </div>
                    <p class="tp-text-grey-2">
                        Elections in Zambia are open to observation and monitoring by international and local organisations.
                    </p>
                </div>
            </div>

        </div>
    </div>
</div>

You said:
Add the header functions and make the icons thin

ChatGPT said:
HTML
<div class="tp-feature-area tp-bg-grey-3 pt-100 pb-60">
    <div class="container">

        <!-- Section Header -->
        <div class="row justify-content-center mb-60">
            <div class="col-lg-8 text-center">
                <span class="tp-section-subtitle">Our Mandate</span>
                <h2 class="tp-section-title">Functions of the Commission</h2>
                <p class="tp-text-grey-2 mt-15">
                    The Electoral Commission is mandated to deliver credible, transparent, and inclusive
                    electoral processes in accordance with the law.
                </p>
            </div>
        </div>

        <div class="row">

            <!-- Delimitation -->
            <div class="col-lg-4 col-md-6">
                <div class="tp-feature-item tpshake-wrap mb-30 wow fadeInLeft" data-wow-delay=".2s" data-wow-duration=".9s">
                    <div class="tp-feature-title d-flex align-items-center mb-20">
                        <span class="tp-feature-icon tpshake mr-30">
                            <i class="ri-map-2-line ri-fw ri-lg"></i>
                        </span>
                        <h4 class="ls-m-2 lh-32">Delimitation</h4>
                    </div>
                    <p class="tp-text-grey-2">
                        Mapping and re-aligning of electoral boundaries to ensure fair representation.
                    </p>
                </div>
            </div>

            <!-- Voter Registration -->
            <div class="col-lg-4 col-md-6">
                <div class="tp-feature-item tpshake-wrap mb-30 wow fadeInLeft" data-wow-delay=".3s" data-wow-duration=".9s">
                    <div class="tp-feature-title d-flex align-items-center mb-20">
                        <span class="tp-feature-icon tpshake mr-30">
                            <i class="ri-user-add-line ri-fw ri-lg"></i>
                        </span>
                        <h4 class="ls-m-2 lh-32">Voter Registration</h4>
                    </div>
                    <p class="tp-text-grey-2">
                        Continuous voter registration as provided for under the Electoral Act.
                    </p>
                </div>
            </div>

            <!-- Voter Education -->
            <div class="col-lg-4 col-md-6">
                <div class="tp-feature-item tpshake-wrap mb-30 wow fadeInLeft" data-wow-delay=".4s" data-wow-duration=".9s">
                    <div class="tp-feature-title d-flex align-items-center mb-20">
                        <span class="tp-feature-icon tpshake mr-30">
                            <i class="ri-book-open-line ri-fw ri-lg"></i>
                        </span>
                        <h4 class="ls-m-2 lh-32">Voter Education</h4>
                    </div>
                    <p class="tp-text-grey-2">
                        Provision of voter education in line with Section 80 of the Electoral Process Act No. 35 of 2016.
                    </p>
                </div>
            </div>

            <!-- Elections -->
            <div class="col-lg-4 col-md-6">
                <div class="tp-feature-item tpshake-wrap mb-30 wow fadeInLeft" data-wow-delay=".5s" data-wow-duration=".9s">
                    <div class="tp-feature-title d-flex align-items-center mb-20">
                        <span class="tp-feature-icon tpshake mr-30">
                            <i class="ri-checkbox-blank-circle-line ri-fw ri-lg"></i>
                        </span>
                        <h4 class="ls-m-2 lh-32">Elections</h4>
                    </div>
                    <p class="tp-text-grey-2">
                        Administration of elections for President, Members of Parliament, Mayors, Council Chairpersons, and Councillors.
                    </p>
                </div>
            </div>

            <!-- Conflict Management -->
            <div class="col-lg-4 col-md-6">
                <div class="tp-feature-item tpshake-wrap mb-30 wow fadeInLeft" data-wow-delay=".6s" data-wow-duration=".9s">
                    <div class="tp-feature-title d-flex align-items-center mb-20">
                        <span class="tp-feature-icon tpshake mr-30">
                            <i class="ri-shield-line ri-fw ri-lg"></i>
                        </span>
                        <h4 class="ls-m-2 lh-32">Conflict Management</h4>
                    </div>
                    <p class="tp-text-grey-2">
                        Managing electoral conflicts and resolving disputes as mandated by law.
                    </p>
                </div>
            </div>

            <!-- Accreditation -->
            <div class="col-lg-4 col-md-6">
                <div class="tp-feature-item tpshake-wrap mb-30 wow fadeInLeft" data-wow-delay=".7s" data-wow-duration=".9s">
                    <div class="tp-feature-title d-flex align-items-center mb-20">
                        <span class="tp-feature-icon tpshake mr-30">
                            <i class="ri-group-line ri-fw ri-lg"></i>
                        </span>
                        <h4 class="ls-m-2 lh-32">Accreditation</h4>
                    </div>
                    <p class="tp-text-grey-2">
                        Accreditation of local and international observers and monitors for electoral processes.
                    </p>
                </div>
            </div>

        </div>
    </div>
</div>

Notes (implementation-friendly)

  • Thin icons: Remix *-line icons are already stroke-style (thin). No filled icons used.

  • You can globally control icon weight/size with CSS:

CSS
.tp-feature-icon i {
    font-size: 56px;
    font-weight: 300;
    color: var(--tp-theme-primary);
}

If you want:

  • hover color transitions,

  • icon backgrounds with soft blur/glass effect,

  • or a Blade loop from a config/JSON array,

say the word and I’ll wire it cleanly.

ChatGPT said:

Add text alignment to the paragraph class or target it inside the feature items.

✅ Option 1 — Center all feature paragraphs (Recommended)

CSS
.tp-feature-item .tp-text-grey-2 {
    text-align: center;
}

✅ Option 2 — Center everything inside each card

If you want title + paragraph centered:

CSS
.tp-feature-item {
    text-align: center;
}

.tp-feature-title {
    justify-content: center;
}

.tp-feature-icon {
    margin-right: 15px; /* optional spacing */
}

✅ Option 3 — Only center the header paragraph (you already almost have it)

You already added text-center to the column, so if it’s not working:

CSS
.tp-section-title + .tp-text-grey-2 {
    text-align: center;
}

🔥 Clean Production Choice

Use this combo:

CSS
/* Header paragraph */
.tp-section-title + .tp-text-grey-2 {
    text-align: center;
}

/* Feature card paragraphs */
.tp-feature-item .tp-text-grey-2 {
    text-align: center;
}

If you want, I can also:

  • vertically center icons + titles,

  • convert cards to fully centered modern ECZ-style tiles,

  • or make them responsive stack-center on mobile.

Just tell me.

We collaborate with you to bring your <span class="tp-text-grey-1 text-italic fw-400 tp-ff-heading">vision to life.</span> </h2> <p class="mb-30">At Budgeto, we empower success through tailored strategic solutions. From innovative consulting to business development and monitoring, we navigate the complexities of the business landscape with you.</p> <p class="mb-55">Our team is dedicated to crafting strategies that align with vision, consulting services that address challenges, & driving business development for expansion. We monitor your operations with precision, ensuring continuous</p> <a href="about.html" class="tp-btn-xl d-inline-block lh-0 tp-round-24 fs-16 tp-bg-theme-primary ls-0 tp-btn-switch-animation tp-text-common-white hover-text-white fw-500"> <span class="d-flex align-items-center justify-content-center"> <span class="btn-text"> Discover more us</span> <span class="btn-icon"><i class="fa-sharp mb-15">Establish year</h4> <p class="mb-20">This year is significant because it provides insight into the history and experience of the consulting firm.</p> <span class="tp-border-bottom d-block ChatGPT said:

Below is a clean adaptation of the About section content tailored specifically for the Electoral Commission of Zambia (ECZ), followed by three realistic, human-centred image prompts suitable for the layout you’re using.

✅ Adapted “About the Commission” Content (ECZ)

Section Subtitle

About the Electoral Commission of Zambia

Main Heading

Safeguarding democracy through transparent, credible, and inclusive electoral processes.

Body Copy (replace the consulting content)

HTML
<p class="mb-30">
    The Electoral Commission of Zambia (ECZ) is an independent constitutional body
    established to manage and supervise the conduct of elections and referenda
    in Zambia in a fair, transparent, and impartial manner.
</p>

<p class="mb-55">
    Guided by the Constitution and the Electoral Process Act, the Commission is
    responsible for voter registration, voter education, delimitation of
    constituencies, accreditation of observers, and the administration of
    national and local government elections. ECZ works closely with stakeholders
    to promote peaceful participation and strengthen public confidence in the
    democratic process.
</p>

Button CTA

HTML
<span class="btn-text">Learn More About ECZ</span>

Counter / Highlight Box (replace “Growth advice”)

HTML
<h2 class="fs-70">
    <span data-purecounter-duration="1" data-purecounter-end="1996" class="purecounter">0</span>
</h2>
<p class="fw-500 fs-18 ls-m-2 tp-text-grey-2">
    Established to serve Zambia’s<br> democratic process
</p>

(1996 is commonly referenced for ECZ’s establishment — adjust if needed)

Bottom Feature Heading

HTML
<h3 class="mb-35">
    Building trust in <span class="tp-ff-heading tp-text-grey-1 text-italic fw-400">
    electoral governance</span>
</h3>

Bottom Feature List (replace consulting bullets)

HTML
<ul>
    <li>Independent and impartial election management</li>
    <li>Continuous voter registration and education</li>
    <li>Transparent and credible electoral processes</li>
    <li>Peaceful conflict resolution and dispute management</li>
    <li>Stakeholder engagement and public accountability</li>
    <li>Accreditation of local and international observers</li>
</ul>

“Established Year” Card

HTML
<h4 class="fs-24 mb-15">Year Established</h4>
<p class="mb-20">
    The establishment of the Commission marked a critical step in strengthening
    Zambia’s democratic institutions and electoral integrity.
</p>

🎨 Three Realistic Image Prompts (Human-Centred, ECZ-Appropriate)

Image Prompt 1 — Civic Participation

Young and middle-aged Zambian voters standing in an orderly queue at a polling station, natural daylight, people holding voter cards, diverse clothing and expressions, calm and hopeful atmosphere, documentary photography style, high realism, shallow depth of field, Lusaka setting

Image Prompt 2 — Electoral Administration

Professional Zambian election officials working inside a modern ECZ office, reviewing voter registers and electoral maps, neutral expressions, official attire, soft natural lighting, realistic corporate documentary style, focus on transparency and diligence

Image Prompt 3 — Community Engagement

ECZ voter education outreach in a rural Zambian community, an official explaining voting procedures to attentive citizens under a tree, mixed ages and genders, warm natural light, authentic African environment, candid human-focused realism

If you want, I can:

  • align these images precisely to left/right thumbnail ratios,

  • add a green ECZ gradient overlay (#0d9153) to the prompts, or

  • optimize them for hero vs about-section cropping.

Just say the word.

2/2

ChatGPT said:

Here is a fully replaced ECZ-ready counter section using your old site statistics and keeping your structure, PureCounter, and layout intact.

I’ve also corrected labels and aligned them to ECZ context.

✅ ECZ Quick Stats Counter Section (Ready to Paste)

HTML
<div class="tp-counter-area tp-bg-grey-4 pt-90 pb-75 p-relative z-index-1">
    <div class="container-fluid">

        <!-- Section Heading -->
        <div class="row justify-content-center mb-60">
            <div class="col-lg-8 text-center">
                <h2 class="tp-section-title">Quick Statistics</h2>
                <p class="tp-text-grey-2 mt-15">
                    National electoral statistics as at 25 February 2025
                </p>
            </div>
        </div>

        <div class="row">

            <!-- Registered Voters -->
            <div class="col-xl-2 col-lg-4 col-md-6">
                <div class="tp-counter-wrap d-flex align-items-center justify-content-xl-center mb-20">
                    <span class="mr-35">
                        <i class="ri-group-line fs-40 tp-text-theme"></i>
                    </span>
                    <div>
                        <h2 class="fs-60 ls-m-3 mb-0">
                            <span data-purecounter-duration="2" data-purecounter-end="7073513" class="purecounter">0</span>
                        </h2>
                        <span class="fw-500 tp-text-grey-1 fs-16">Registered Voters</span>
                    </div>
                </div>
            </div>

            <!-- Provinces -->
            <div class="col-xl-2 col-lg-4 col-md-6">
                <div class="tp-counter-wrap d-flex align-items-center justify-content-xl-center mb-20">
                    <span class="mr-35">
                        <i class="ri-map-pin-line fs-40 tp-text-theme"></i>
                    </span>
                    <div>
                        <h2 class="fs-60 ls-m-3 mb-0">
                            <span data-purecounter-duration="2" data-purecounter-end="10" class="purecounter">0</span>
                        </h2>
                        <span class="fw-500 tp-text-grey-1 fs-16">Provinces</span>
                    </div>
                </div>
            </div>

            <!-- Districts -->
            <div class="col-xl-2 col-lg-4 col-md-6">
                <div class="tp-counter-wrap d-flex align-items-center justify-content-xl-center mb-20">
                    <span class="mr-35">
                        <i class="ri-building-line fs-40 tp-text-theme"></i>
                    </span>
                    <div>
                        <h2 class="fs-60 ls-m-3 mb-0">
                            <span data-purecounter-duration="2" data-purecounter-end="116" class="purecounter">0</span>
                        </h2>
                        <span class="fw-500 tp-text-grey-1 fs-16">Districts</span>
                    </div>
                </div>
            </div>

            <!-- Constituencies -->
            <div class="col-xl-2 col-lg-4 col-md-6">
                <div class="tp-counter-wrap d-flex align-items-center justify-content-xl-center mb-20">
                    <span class="mr-35">
                        <i class="ri-map-2-line fs-40 tp-text-theme"></i>
                    </span>
                    <div>
                        <h2 class="fs-60 ls-m-3 mb-0">
                            <span data-purecounter-duration="2" data-purecounter-end="156" class="purecounter">0</span>
                        </h2>
                        <span class="fw-500 tp-text-grey-1 fs-16">Constituencies</span>
                    </div>
                </div>
            </div>

            <!-- Wards -->
            <div class="col-xl-2 col-lg-4 col-md-6">
                <div class="tp-counter-wrap d-flex align-items-center justify-content-xl-center mb-20">
                    <span class="mr-35">
                        <i class="ri-layout-grid-line fs-40 tp-text-theme"></i>
                    </span>
                    <div>
                        <h2 class="fs-60 ls-m-3 mb-0">
                            <span data-purecounter-duration="2" data-purecounter-end="1858" class="purecounter">0</span>
                        </h2>
                        <span class="fw-500 tp-text-grey-1 fs-16">Wards</span>
                    </div>
                </div>
            </div>

            <!-- Polling Stations -->
            <div class="col-xl-2 col-lg-4 col-md-6">
                <div class="tp-counter-wrap d-flex align-items-center justify-content-xl-center mb-20">
                    <span class="mr-35">
                        <i class="ri-checkbox-circle-line fs-40 tp-text-theme"></i>
                    </span>
                    <div>
                        <h2 class="fs-60 ls-m-3 mb-0">
                            <span data-purecounter-duration="2" data-purecounter-end="12200" class="purecounter">0</span>
                        </h2>
                        <span class="fw-500 tp-text-grey-1 fs-16">Polling Stations</span>
                    </div>
                </div>
            </div>

        </div>
    </div>
</div>

✅ Why This Version Works Better

  • Matches ECZ real-world structure

  • Uses Remix icons (thin + modern government look)

  • Keeps PureCounter animation

  • Scales better (6 stats instead of forcing 4)

  • Clean for responsive grids

If you want next, I can:

  • turn this into a Zambia map stats visual section,

  • add green ECZ gradient overlay background,

  • or animate counters on scroll only (performance optimized).

HTML
<div class="tp-counter-area tp-bg-grey-4 pt-90 pb-75 p-relative z-index-1">
    <div class="container-fluid">
        <div class="row">

            <!-- Registered Voters -->
            <div class="col-xl-3 col-lg-6 col-md-6">
                <div class="tp-counter-wrap d-flex align-items-center justify-content-xl-center mb-20">
                    <span class="mr-35">
                        <!-- People / Users Icon -->
                        <svg width="72" height="72" viewBox="0 0 72 72" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">

                            <path d="M12 60C12 48 24 40 36 40C48 40 60 48 60 60" stroke="#1F3130" stroke-width="2"/>
                        </svg>
                    </span>
                    <div>
                        <h2 class="fs-60 ls-m-3 mb-0">
                            <span data-purecounter-duration="1" data-purecounter-end="7073513" class="purecounter">0</span>
                        </h2>
                        <span class="fw-500 tp-text-grey-1 fs-16">Registered Voters</span>
                    </div>
                </div>
            </div>

            <!-- Constituencies -->
            <div class="col-xl-3 col-lg-6 col-md-6">
                <div class="tp-counter-wrap d-flex align-items-center justify-content-xl-center mb-20">
                    <span class="mr-35">
                        <!-- Map / Pin Icon -->
                        <svg width="72" height="72" viewBox="0 0 72 72" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">
                            <path d="M36 2C23 2 12 13 12 26C12 40 36 70 36 70C36 70 60 40 60 26C60 13 49 2 36 2Z" stroke="#1F3130" stroke-width="2"/>
                            <circle cx="36" cy="26" r="6" stroke="#1F3130" stroke-width="2"/>
                        </svg>
                    </span>
                    <div>
                        <h2 class="fs-60 ls-m-3 mb-0">
                            <span data-purecounter-duration="1" data-purecounter-end="156" class="purecounter">0</span>
                        </h2>
                        <span class="fw-500 tp-text-grey-1 fs-16">Constituencies</span>
                    </div>
                </div>
            </div>

            <!-- Wards -->
            <div class="col-xl-3 col-lg-6 col-md-6">
                <div class="tp-counter-wrap d-flex align-items-center justify-content-xl-center mb-20">
                    <span class="mr-35">
                        <!-- Grid / Structure Icon -->
                        <svg width="72" height="72" viewBox="0 0 72 72" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">
                            <rect x="8" y="8" width="20" height="20" stroke="#1F3130" stroke-width="2"/>

        </div>
    </div>
</div>

  2. Constituencies – map pin icon

ChatGPT said:

Here’s a commission-focused adaptation of your FAQ section, keeping the layout, animations, and accordion structure intact, but updating the content to suit a “Commissions & Elections” theme:

HTML
<div class="tp-faq-area pt-140 pb-110">
    <div class="container">
        <div class="row">

            <!-- FAQ Image -->
            <div class="col-lg-6">
                <div class="tp-faq-4-thumb p-relative z-index-1 mb-30">
                    <span class="tp-faq-4-thumb-shape p-absolute"></span>
                    <div class="ml-30 thumb mr-85">
                        <img class="w-100 tp-round-4" src="assets/img/faq/faq-4/thumb.jpg" alt="Commissions FAQ">
                    </div>
                </div>
            </div>

            <!-- FAQ Accordion -->
            <div class="col-lg-6">
                <div class="tp-faq-wrapper tp-faq-4-wrapper mb-30">
                    <h2 class="mb-20 fs-40 lh-1 fs-sm-36 wow img-custom-anim-right" data-wow-duration="1.5s" data-wow-delay="0.2s">
                        Learn about <span class="text-italic fw-400 tp-ff-heading">our election processes.</span>
                    </h2>
                    <p class="mb-40">
                        Here we answer the most common questions about voter registration, polling stations, and commission operations.
                    </p>
                    <div class="accordion" id="commission_faqaccordion">

                        <!-- Question 1 -->
                        <div class="accordion-item expand">
                            <h2 class="accordion-header" id="faq_one">
                                <button class="accordion-button faq-expend tp-faq-btn-2" type="button" data-bs-toggle="collapse" data-bs-target="#faq__collapse_one" aria-expanded="true" aria-controls="faq__collapse_one">
                                    <span>01</span>
                                    How do I register to vote?
                                </button>
                            </h2>
                            <div id="faq__collapse_one" class="accordion-collapse collapse show" aria-labelledby="faq_one" data-bs-parent="#commission_faqaccordion">
                                <div class="accordion-body tp-faq-details-para">
                                    <p>You can register online or at your local registration office. Ensure you have valid identification and proof of residence to complete the registration process.</p>
                                </div>
                            </div>
                        </div>

                        <!-- Question 2 -->
                        <div class="accordion-item">
                            <h2 class="accordion-header" id="faq_two">
                                <button class="accordion-button faq-expend collapsed tp-faq-btn-2" type="button" data-bs-toggle="collapse" data-bs-target="#faq__collapse_two" aria-expanded="false" aria-controls="faq__collapse_two">
                                    <span>02</span>
                                    Where are the polling stations located?
                                </button>
                            </h2>
                            <div id="faq__collapse_two" class="accordion-collapse collapse" aria-labelledby="faq_two" data-bs-parent="#commission_faqaccordion">
                                <div class="accordion-body tp-faq-details-para">
                                    <p>Polling stations are located across all districts and wards. You can find your assigned station on our official website or via SMS notifications once registered.</p>
                                </div>
                            </div>
                        </div>

                        <!-- Question 3 -->
                        <div class="accordion-item">
                            <h2 class="accordion-header" id="faq_three">
                                <button class="accordion-button faq-expend collapsed tp-faq-btn-2" type="button" data-bs-toggle="collapse" data-bs-target="#faq__collapse_three" aria-expanded="false" aria-controls="faq__collapse_three">
                                    <span>03</span>
                                    How are election results verified?
                                </button>
                            </h2>
                            <div id="faq__collapse_three" class="accordion-collapse collapse" aria-labelledby="faq_three" data-bs-parent="#commission_faqaccordion">
                                <div class="accordion-body tp-faq-details-para">
                                    <p>Results are verified through a multi-tier system involving polling station reports, constituency-level tabulations, and independent audits to ensure accuracy and transparency.</p>
                                </div>
                            </div>
                        </div>

                        <!-- Question 4 -->
                        <div class="accordion-item">
                            <h2 class="accordion-header" id="faq_four">
                                <button class="accordion-button faq-expend collapsed tp-faq-btn-2" type="button" data-bs-toggle="collapse" data-bs-target="#faq__collapse_four" aria-expanded="false" aria-controls="faq__collapse_four">
                                    <span>04</span>
                                    What is the role of the commission?
                                </button>
                            </h2>
                            <div id="faq__collapse_four" class="accordion-collapse collapse" aria-labelledby="faq_four" data-bs-parent="#commission_faqaccordion">
                                <div class="accordion-body tp-faq-details-para">
                                    <p>The commission oversees all aspects of elections, including voter registration, polling station management, election monitoring, and announcing official results in accordance with national laws.</p>
                                </div>
                            </div>
                        </div>

                    </div>
                </div>
            </div>

        </div>
    </div>
</div>

✅ Changes made:

  • Updated heading and paragraph to reflect elections and commission.

  • Replaced generic HR questions with commission-specific FAQs:

    1. Voter registration

    2. Polling station locations

    3. Result verification

    4. Commission roles

  • Accordion IDs and button numbers are updated for clarity.

I can also add SVG icons for each FAQ item to visually match the counter section, making the FAQ more interactive.

Do you want me to do that?


You said:
change to background image: .tp-faq-4-thumb-shape { border-radius: 4px; width: 82%; height: 100%; display: inline-block; background: rgb(125, 125, 125); z-index: -1; left: 0; bottom: -30px; }

ChatGPT said:

You can convert .tp-faq-4-thumb-shape from a solid color block to a background image by replacing the background: rgb(125, 125, 125); with a background-image property. Here’s the updated CSS:

CSS
.tp-faq-4-thumb-shape {
  border-radius: 4px;
  width: 82%;
  height: 100%;
  display: inline-block;
  background-image: url('assets/img/faq/faq-4/shape-bg.jpg'); /* replace with your image path */
  background-size: cover;       /* ensures the image covers the entire element */
  background-position: center;  /* centers the image */
  background-repeat: no-repeat; /* prevents tiling */
  z-index: -1;
  left: 0;
  bottom: -30px;
}

✅ Notes:

  • Replace 'assets/img/faq/faq-4/shape-bg.jpg' with the correct path to your image.

  • background-size: cover makes sure the image fills the block, even if it crops slightly.

  • You can also use background-size: contain if you want the whole image visible without cropping.

If you want, I can also make it responsive so the background image adapts nicely on mobile screens. Do you want me to do that?

ChatGPT said:

You can color the SVGs green by changing the stroke attribute of each SVG element. For example, replace stroke="#1F3130" with stroke="#28a745" (a nice green) or any green hex code you prefer. Here's your updated code:

HTML
<div class="tp-counter-area tp-bg-grey-4 pt-90 pb-75 p-relative z-index-1">
    <div class="container-fluid">
        <div class="row">

            <!-- Registered Voters -->
            <div class="col-xl-3 col-lg-6 col-md-6">
                <div class="tp-counter-wrap d-flex align-items-center justify-content-xl-center mb-20">
                    <span class="mr-35">
                        <!-- People / Users Icon -->
                        <svg width="72" height="72" viewBox="0 0 72 72" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">
                            <circle cx="36" cy="24" r="12" stroke="#28a745" stroke-width="2"/>
                            <path d="M12 60C12 48 24 40 36 40C48 40 60 48 60 60" stroke="#28a745" stroke-width="2"/>
                        </svg>
                    </span>
                    <div>
                        <h2 style="color:black;" class="fs-60 ls-m-3 mb-0">
                            <span data-purecounter-duration="1" data-purecounter-end="7073513" class="purecounter">0</span>
                        </h2>
                        <span class="fw-500 tp-text-grey-1 fs-16">Registered Voters</span>
                    </div>
                </div>
            </div>

            <!-- Constituencies -->
            <div class="col-xl-3 col-lg-6 col-md-6">
                <div class="tp-counter-wrap d-flex align-items-center justify-content-xl-center mb-20">
                    <span class="mr-35">
                        <!-- Map / Pin Icon -->
                        <svg width="72" height="72" viewBox="0 0 72 72" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">
                            <path d="M36 2C23 2 12 13 12 26C12 40 36 70 36 70C36 70 60 40 60 26C60 13 49 2 36 2Z" stroke="#28a745" stroke-width="2"/>
                            <circle cx="36" cy="26" r="6" stroke="#28a745" stroke-width="2"/>
                        </svg>
                    </span>
                    <div>
                        <h2 style="color:black;" class="fs-60 ls-m-3 mb-0">
                            <span data-purecounter-duration="1" data-purecounter-end="156" class="purecounter">0</span>
                        </h2>
                        <span class="fw-500 tp-text-grey-1 fs-16">Constituencies</span>
                    </div>
                </div>
            </div>

            <!-- Wards -->
            <div class="col-xl-3 col-lg-6 col-md-6">
                <div class="tp-counter-wrap d-flex align-items-center justify-content-xl-center mb-20">
                    <span class="mr-35">
                        <!-- Grid / Structure Icon -->
                        <svg width="72" height="72" viewBox="0 0 72 72" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">
                            <rect x="8" y="8" width="20" height="20" stroke="#28a745" stroke-width="2"/>
                            <rect x="44" y="8" width="20" height="20" stroke="#28a745" stroke-width="2"/>
                            <rect x="8" y="44" width="20" height="20" stroke="#28a745" stroke-width="2"/>
                            <rect x="44" y="44" width="20" height="20" stroke="#28a745" stroke-width="2"/>
                        </svg>
                    </span>
                    <div>
                        <h2 style="color:black;" class="fs-60 ls-m-3 mb-0">
                            <span data-purecounter-duration="1" data-purecounter-end="1858" class="purecounter">0</span>
                        </h2>
                        <span class="fw-500 tp-text-grey-1 fs-16">Wards</span>
                    </div>
                </div>
            </div>

            <!-- Polling Stations -->
            <div class="col-xl-3 col-lg-6 col-md-6">
                <div class="tp-counter-wrap d-flex align-items-center justify-content-xl-center mb-20">
                    <span class="mr-35">
                        <!-- Check / Polling Icon -->
                        <svg width="72" height="72" viewBox="0 0 72 72" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">
                            <circle cx="36" cy="36" r="34" stroke="#28a745" stroke-width="2"/>
