You said:
create image prompts for each
2/2

ChatGPT said:

Here are high-quality generation prompts you can use (Midjourney, DALL-E, Stable Diffusion, Leonardo, etc.).
Each is tailored to look like official electoral / civic institutional imagery, not stock-blog style.

1️⃣ Campaign Regalia By-Election Statement

Prompt

Code
Professional documentary photograph of peaceful political campaign activity in an African urban constituency, voters and party supporters wearing mixed neutral and campaign colors, election posters on street poles, community buildings in background, natural daylight, realistic photography, high detail, civic process atmosphere, 50mm lens, shallow depth of field, editorial style, no logos, no text

Alternative (Illustrative / Hero Section Style)

Code
Clean vector illustration of democratic election campaign environment in Africa, diverse citizens standing together peacefully, subtle national colors, modern civic design style, soft shadows, high resolution, institutional government communication style

2️⃣ ECZ Warehouse Tender – Infrastructure / Procurement

Prompt

Code
Modern pre-fabricated warehouse facility in Southern Africa, clean industrial compound, paved yard, clear sky, security fencing, logistics loading bay, realistic architecture photography, ultra realistic, professional infrastructure documentation style, high dynamic range, no branding, no text

Alternative (Corporate / Render Style)

Code
3D architectural render of modern prefabricated logistics warehouse, steel structure, clean white and grey panels, African environment lighting, professional procurement presentation style, ultra sharp, high resolution

3️⃣ Voter Registration Countdown

Prompt

Code
African citizens registering to vote at official registration center, electoral officers assisting voters, biometric registration desk with computers and cameras, organized queue, bright natural lighting, realistic documentary photography, civic participation theme, highly detailed, professional government communications style

Alternative (Hero Banner Style)

Code
Symbolic image of civic participation, diverse African citizens standing confidently, subtle ballot box symbol in background, bright hopeful lighting, modern institutional photography style, shallow depth of field, ultra high resolution

4️⃣ Mass Voter Registration Deployment Plan

Prompt

Code
Election logistics preparation scene in Africa, election staff organizing ballot materials and equipment in warehouse, labeled boxes, laptops, planning boards, teamwork environment, professional documentary photography, realistic lighting, high detail, official electoral operations style

Alternative (Strategic / Concept Visual)

Code
Top view strategic planning table with maps, logistics documents, tablets, voting location markers, African election operations planning concept, professional government presentation photography, ultra realistic, high resolution

Recommended Image Specs (For Your Website Sections)

Use:

  • Hero / Section Image: 1920 × 1080

  • Card / Blog Thumbnail: 800 × 600

  • Retina Ready: Generate at 2× (e.g., 1600 × 1200)

If you want, I can:

  • Convert these into exact prompts optimized for Midjourney v6, or

  • Convert them into OpenAI image_gen ready prompts, or

  • Generate consistent style prompts so all 4 images look like one photoshoot.

HTML

ChatGPT said:
HTML
<style>
/* Base icon color */
.tp-footer-bottom-social ul li a {
    display: inline-flex;
    align-items: center;
    justify-content: center;
    transition: all 0.3s ease;
}

/* Red icons */
.tp-footer-bottom-social ul li:nth-child(1) a,
.tp-footer-bottom-social ul li:nth-child(3) a {
    color: #ff2d2d;
}

/* Orange icons */
.tp-footer-bottom-social ul li:nth-child(2) a,
.tp-footer-bottom-social ul li:nth-child(4) a {
    color: #ff7a00;
}

/* Hover effect */
.tp-footer-bottom-social ul li a:hover {
    transform: translateY(-2px);
    opacity: 0.85;
}
</style>

Give me more hero prompts like this: ⌘J modern Young good looking Zambian adults lining up at a bustling Lusaka polling station, laughing and talking naturally, some holding voter cards casually, wearing realistic colorful everyday clothing, sunlight streaming through windows creating natural highlights, candid and dynamic composition, human-focused documentary realism. ChatGPT said:

Here are more high-quality hero image prompts in the same documentary-realism, civic, Zambian context you’re using. They’re written to work well for wide hero banners, landing pages, and campaign sites.

1. First-Time Voters – Optimism & Energy

Modern young Zambian first-time voters gathered outside a polling station in Lusaka, smiling and chatting confidently, some checking voter cards on their phones, others gesturing animatedly, wearing authentic everyday African urban fashion, bright natural morning light casting soft shadows, candid and hopeful atmosphere, documentary realism.

Natural African daylight color grading, ultra-realistic skin tones, shallow depth of field, environmental storytelling, high-end photojournalism style, wide hero banner composition.

2. Inclusive Democracy – All Generations

Diverse group of Zambian citizens of different ages standing together at a community polling center, elderly voters, middle-aged professionals, and young adults interacting naturally, subtle smiles and respectful gestures, realistic civic setting with signage and queues, warm sunlight filtering through trees.

Authentic African color tones, cinematic realism, detailed textures, balanced depth of field, human-centered composition, wide cinematic hero image.

3. Urban Civic Participation

Busy urban polling station in Lusaka with young professionals and students lining up calmly, modern city background with trees and buildings, casual conversations and relaxed body language, realistic clothing and accessories, midday sunlight creating natural highlights and contrast.

Ultra-realistic documentary photography, natural African lighting, crisp textures, civic authenticity, wide panoramic hero banner framing.

4. Confidence in the Electoral Process

Zambian voters confidently approaching a polling desk inside a well-organized voting hall, election officials assisting calmly, natural human expressions, authentic interior lighting with sunlight through windows, sense of trust and order, candid moments captured naturally.

High-end documentary realism, accurate skin tones, soft shadows, shallow depth of field, institutional yet human atmosphere, wide hero composition.

5. Community & Unity

Zambian community members gathered outside a polling station, people greeting each other warmly, subtle handshakes and laughter, trees and school buildings in the background, realistic civic environment, late-afternoon golden light adding warmth.

Cinematic African daylight grading, ultra-realistic detail, emotionally engaging realism, human-focused storytelling, wide hero banner layout.

6. Women in Democracy

Confident young Zambian women standing in line to vote, expressive faces and natural body language, colorful everyday clothing, sunlight illuminating faces softly, calm yet empowering atmosphere, authentic African civic setting.

Documentary-style realism, rich natural skin tones, shallow depth of field, high-resolution photographic quality, wide banner hero framing.

7. Early Morning Voting

Early morning at a Lusaka polling station, light mist and soft sunrise glow, voters quietly lining up with calm determination, natural candid interactions, realistic uniforms and signage, peaceful and responsible civic mood.

Ultra-realistic photography, natural African sunrise lighting, cinematic realism, subtle color grading, wide hero image composition.

8. Youth Engagement & Future

Young Zambian adults discussing civic participation while waiting to vote, expressive faces, relaxed confidence, realistic urban background, sunlight creating lens highlights, spontaneous and documentary-style framing.

High-end photojournalism, natural tones, detailed textures, emotional authenticity, panoramic hero layout.

If you want, I can:

  • Tune these specifically for ECZ branding

  • Add green-gradient overlay instructions

  • Optimize them for Midjourney / DALL·E / Grok

  • Create matching section-image prompts (not just hero)

ChatGPT said:

Here’s the same structure and layout, with content adapted for an Electoral Commission context (ECZ-style civic, voter education, participation, transparency).
No classes, structure, or design behavior changed — only text.

HTML
<div class="tp-hero-area fix p-relative">
   <div class="swiper tp-hero-slider-zoom">
      <div class="swiper-wrapper">

         <!-- Slide 1 -->
         <div class="swiper-slide">
            <div class="tp-hero-singles p-relative">
               <div class="tp-hero-bg bg-position" data-background="assets/img/hero/bg.jpg"></div>
               <div class="container">
                  <div class="row align-items-end">
                     <div class="col-lg-7 tp-hero-spacing">
                        <div class="tp-hero-content-wrapper">
                           <h2 class="tp-hero-title text fw-600 fs-70 fs-lg-50 fs-xs-34 ls-m-3 tp-text-common-white lh-1 mb-25">
                              Strengthening democracy through
                              <span class="tp-ff-heading text-italic tp-text-theme-secondary fw-400">credible elections.</span>
                           </h2>

                           <p class="text2 mb-50 tp-text-grey-3">
                              The Commission is committed to delivering free, fair and transparent elections.
                              We provide voter education, maintain an accurate voters’ register and ensure
                              every eligible citizen has the opportunity to participate in the democratic process.
                           </p>

                           <div class="tp-hero-btn text3">
                              <a href="register.html" class="tp-btn-xl d-inline-block lh-0 tp-round-24 fs-16 tp-bg-common-white ls-0 tp-btn-switch-animation tp-text-theme-primary fw-500">
                                 <span class="d-flex align-items-center justify-content-center">
                                    <span class="btn-text"> Check Voter Registration </span>
                                    <span class="btn-icon"><i class="fa-sharp fa-regular fa-arrow-right"></i></span>
                                    <span class="btn-icon"><i class="fa-sharp fa-regular fa-arrow-right"></i></span>
                                 </span> 
                              </a>
                           </div>
                        </div>
                     </div>

                     <div class="col-lg-5">
                        <div class="tp-hero-contact tp-round-4 mb-100 tp-slider-para-home">
                           <p class="tp-text-grey-3 mb-25">
                              Get official information about voter registration, polling stations,
                              election dates and electoral guidelines.
                           </p>
                           <div class="tp-hero-contact-btn">
                              <a href="contact.html" class="fw-500 tp-text-common-white d-flex justify-content-between align-items-center">
                                 Contact the Commission
                                 <i class="fa-sharp fa-regular fa-arrow-right"></i>
                              </a>
                           </div>
                        </div>
                     </div>

                  </div>
               </div>
            </div>
         </div>

         <!-- Slide 2 -->
         <div class="swiper-slide">
            <div class="tp-hero-singles p-relative">
               <div class="tp-hero-bg bg-position" data-background="assets/img/hero/bg-2.jpg"></div>
               <div class="container">
                  <div class="row align-items-end">
                     <div class="col-lg-7 tp-hero-spacing">
                        <div class="tp-hero-content-wrapper">
                           <h2 class="tp-hero-title text fw-600 fs-70 fs-lg-50 fs-xs-34 ls-m-3 tp-text-common-white lh-1 mb-25">
                              Empowering citizens through
                              <span class="tp-ff-heading text-italic tp-text-theme-secondary fw-400">voter education.</span>
                           </h2>

                           <p class="text2 mb-50 tp-text-grey-3">
                              Access reliable voter education resources, learn how to participate in elections,
                              and stay informed about electoral processes designed to strengthen democratic governance.
                           </p>

                           <div class="tp-hero-btn text3">
                              <a href="voter-education.html" class="tp-btn-xl d-inline-block lh-0 tp-round-24 fs-16 tp-bg-common-white ls-0 tp-btn-switch-animation tp-text-theme-primary fw-500">
                                 <span class="d-flex align-items-center justify-content-center">
                                    <span class="btn-text"> Explore Voter Education </span>
                                    <span class="btn-icon"><i class="fa-sharp fa-regular fa-arrow-right"></i></span>
                                    <span class="btn-icon"><i class="fa-sharp fa-regular fa-arrow-right"></i></span>
                                 </span> 
                              </a>
                           </div>
                        </div>
                     </div>

                     <div class="col-lg-5">
                        <div class="tp-hero-contact tp-round-4 mb-100 tp-slider-para-home">
                           <p class="tp-text-grey-3 mb-25">
                              Find resources, guidelines and announcements related to upcoming elections
                              and electoral activities nationwide.
                           </p>
                           <div class="tp-hero-contact-btn">
                              <a href="news.html" class="fw-500 tp-text-common-white d-flex justify-content-between align-items-center">
                                 Latest Announcements
                                 <i class="fa-sharp fa-regular fa-arrow-right"></i>
                              </a>
                           </div>
                        </div>
                     </div>

                  </div>
               </div>
            </div>
         </div>

         <!-- Slide 3 -->
         <div class="swiper-slide">
            <div class="tp-hero-singles p-relative">
               <div class="tp-hero-bg bg-position" data-background="assets/img/hero/bg-3.jpg"></div>
               <div class="container">
                  <div class="row align-items-end">
                     <div class="col-lg-7 tp-hero-spacing">
                        <div class="tp-hero-content-wrapper">
                           <h2 class="tp-hero-title text fw-600 fs-70 fs-lg-50 fs-xs-34 ls-m-3 tp-text-common-white lh-1 mb-25">
                              Transparent processes for
                              <span class="tp-ff-heading text-italic tp-text-theme-secondary fw-400">trusted outcomes.</span>
                           </h2>

                           <p class="text2 mb-50 tp-text-grey-3">
                              The Commission ensures transparency, accountability and integrity throughout
                              the electoral cycle, from voter registration to results management and certification.
                           </p>

                           <div class="tp-hero-btn text3">
                              <a href="results.html" class="tp-btn-xl d-inline-block lh-0 tp-round-24 fs-16 tp-bg-common-white ls-0 tp-btn-switch-animation tp-text-theme-primary fw-500">
                                 <span class="d-flex align-items-center justify-content-center">
                                    <span class="btn-text"> View Election Results </span>
                                    <span class="btn-icon"><i class="fa-sharp fa-regular fa-arrow-right"></i></span>
                                    <span class="btn-icon"><i class="fa-sharp fa-regular fa-arrow-right"></i></span>
                                 </span> 
                              </a>
                           </div>
                        </div>
                     </div>

                     <div class="col-lg-5">
                        <div class="tp-hero-contact tp-round-4 mb-100 tp-slider-para-home">
                           <p class="tp-text-grey-3 mb-25">
                              Access official election results, publications and transparency reports
                              from the Commission.
                           </p>
                           <div class="tp-hero-contact-btn">
                              <a href="publications.html" class="fw-500 tp-text-common-white d-flex justify-content-between align-items-center">
                                 View Publications
                                 <i class="fa-sharp fa-regular fa-arrow-right"></i>
                              </a>
                           </div>
                        </div>
                     </div>

                  </div>
               </div>
            </div>
         </div>

      </div>
   </div>

   <div class="tp-hero-slider-navigate p-absolute">
      <div class="container">
         <div class="row">
            <div class="col-12">
               <div class="tp-hero-slider-pagenation"></div>
            </div>
         </div>
      </div>
   </div>
</div>

                        <!-- SEARCH -->
                        <div class="tp-header-search">

                              href="register.html"
                              class="tp-btn-lg d-inline-block lh-0 tp-round-24 fs-16 ls-0 tp-btn-switch-animation tp-text-theme-primary fw-500">
                              <span class="d-flex align-items-center justify-content-center">

                              </span>

                     </div>

               </div>

            </div>
         </div>
      </div>
   </div>
</header>

  • 🇿🇲 English (Default / Official)

  • 🇿🇲 Bemba

  • 🇿🇲 Nyanja

  • 🇿🇲 Tonga

  • 🇿🇲 Lozi

  • 🇿🇲 Kaonde

  • 🇿🇲 Lunda

  • 🇿🇲 Luvale

  • 🇫🇷 French (Regional / SADC / Diplomatic)

HTML
<div class="col-lg-3">
   <div class="d-flex justify-content-end">
      <div class="tp-header-top-lang tp-header-3-lang p-relative d-inline-block">

         <!-- Selected Language -->
         <div>
            <img class="rounded-circle mr-10" data-width="40"
                 src="assets/img/flags/zm.png" alt="English Flag">
            <span class="tp-header-top-selected-lang fs-16 fw-400 tp-text-grey-3 p-relative">
               English
            </span>
         </div>

         <!-- Language List -->
         <ul class="tp-header-top-lang-list">

            <li>
               <img class="rounded-circle" data-width="30"
                    src="assets/img/flags/zm.png" alt="English">
               English
            </li>

            <li>
               <img class="rounded-circle" data-width="30"
                    src="assets/img/flags/zm.png" alt="Bemba">
               Bemba
            </li>

            <li>
               <img class="rounded-circle" data-width="30"
                    src="assets/img/flags/zm.png" alt="Nyanja">
               Nyanja
            </li>

            <li>
               <img class="rounded-circle" data-width="30"
                    src="assets/img/flags/zm.png" alt="Tonga">
               Tonga
            </li>

            <li>
               <img class="rounded-circle" data-width="30"
                    src="assets/img/flags/zm.png" alt="Lozi">
               Lozi
            </li>

            <li>
               <img class="rounded-circle" data-width="30"
                    src="assets/img/flags/zm.png" alt="Kaonde">
               Kaonde
            </li>

            <li>
               <img class="rounded-circle" data-width="30"
                    src="assets/img/flags/zm.png" alt="Lunda">
               Lunda
            </li>

            <li>
               <img class="rounded-circle" data-width="30"
                    src="assets/img/flags/zm.png" alt="Luvale">
               Luvale
            </li>

            <li>
               <img class="rounded-circle" data-width="30"
                    src="assets/img/flags/fr.png" alt="French">
               French
            </li>

         </ul>
      </div>
   </div>
</div>

✅ Recommended Flag Asset Naming

Place in:

Code
assets/img/flags/

Suggested files:

Code
zm.png  (Zambia flag)
fr.png  (France flag)
gb.png  (Optional if you want UK flag for English alt)

ChatGPT said:

Here is your team section updated with the Commission members, keeping your exact structure, styling, and layout — only replacing the content with the Commission data.

You can swap image paths later when you have official portraits.

HTML
<div class="tp-team-area pt-135 pb-100">
   <div class="container">
      <div class="row">
         <div class="col-lg-12">
            <div class="tp-team-2-title-wrap text-center mb-50">
               <span class="fw-500 fs-16 tp-text-grey-1 mb-5 d-inline-block">Members of The Commission</span>
               <h2 class="mb-20 fs-xl-40 fs-sm-36 wow img-custom-anim-right" data-wow-duration="1.5s" data-wow-delay="0.2s">
                  Electoral Commission <span class="text-italic fw-400 tp-ff-heading">Leadership</span>
               </h2>
            </div>
         </div>

         <!-- Chairperson -->
         <div class="col-lg-4 col-md-6">
            <div class="tp-team-2-wrap text-center mb-30">
               <div class="tp-team-2-thumb fix tp-round-4 mb-25 p-relative">
                  <img class="w-100 tp-round-4" src="assets/img/team/commission/chairperson.jpg" alt="Chairperson">
                  <div class="tp-team-2-social">
                     <ul class="list-wrap">
                        <li><a href="#"><i class="fa-brands fa-linkedin"></i><span></span></a></li>
                     </ul>
                     <div class="social-toggle-icon">
                        <i class="fa-sharp fa-light fa-share-nodes"></i>
                     </div>
                  </div>
               </div>
               <div class="tp-team-2-content">
                  <h4 class="fw-500 fs-24 mb-0">
                     <a class="underline-black" href="commission-zaloumis.html">Mrs Mwangala Zaloumis</a>
                  </h4>
                  <span class="tp-text-grey-1">Chairperson</span>
               </div>
            </div>
         </div>

         <!-- Commissioner -->
         <div class="col-lg-4 col-md-6">
            <div class="tp-team-2-wrap text-center mb-30">
               <div class="tp-team-2-thumb fix tp-round-4 mb-25 p-relative">
                  <img class="w-100 tp-round-4" src="assets/img/team/commission/mukanda.jpg" alt="Commissioner">
                  <div class="tp-team-2-social">
                     <ul class="list-wrap">
                        <li><a href="#"><i class="fa-brands fa-linkedin"></i><span></span></a></li>
                     </ul>
                     <div class="social-toggle-icon">
                        <i class="fa-sharp fa-light fa-share-nodes"></i>
                     </div>
                  </div>
               </div>
               <div class="tp-team-2-content">
                  <h4 class="fw-500 fs-24 mb-0">
                     <a class="underline-black" href="commission-mukanda.html">Maj. Gen. Vincent Mukanda (Rtd)</a>
                  </h4>
                  <span class="tp-text-grey-1">Commissioner</span>
               </div>
            </div>
         </div>

         <!-- Commissioner -->
         <div class="col-lg-4 col-md-6">
            <div class="tp-team-2-wrap text-center mb-30">
               <div class="tp-team-2-thumb fix tp-round-4 mb-25 p-relative">
                  <img class="w-100 tp-round-4" src="assets/img/team/commission/mutiti.jpg" alt="Commissioner">
                  <div class="tp-team-2-social">
                     <ul class="list-wrap">
                        <li><a href="#"><i class="fa-brands fa-linkedin"></i><span></span></a></li>
                     </ul>
                     <div class="social-toggle-icon">
                        <i class="fa-sharp fa-light fa-share-nodes"></i>
                     </div>
                  </div>
               </div>
               <div class="tp-team-2-content">
                  <h4 class="fw-500 fs-24 mb-0">
                     <a class="underline-black" href="commission-mutiti.html">Ms. Ndiyoi M. Mutiti</a>
                  </h4>
                  <span class="tp-text-grey-1">Commissioner</span>
               </div>
            </div>
         </div>

         <!-- Commissioner -->
         <div class="col-lg-4 col-md-6">
            <div class="tp-team-2-wrap text-center mb-30">
               <div class="tp-team-2-thumb fix tp-round-4 mb-25 p-relative">
                  <img class="w-100 tp-round-4" src="assets/img/team/commission/chipenzi.jpg" alt="Commissioner">
                  <div class="tp-team-2-social">
                     <ul class="list-wrap">
                        <li><a href="#"><i class="fa-brands fa-linkedin"></i><span></span></a></li>
                     </ul>
                     <div class="social-toggle-icon">
                        <i class="fa-sharp fa-light fa-share-nodes"></i>
                     </div>
                  </div>
               </div>
               <div class="tp-team-2-content">
                  <h4 class="fw-500 fs-24 mb-0">
                     <a class="underline-black" href="commission-chipenzi.html">Mr. McDonald G. Chipenzi</a>
                  </h4>
                  <span class="tp-text-grey-1">Commissioner</span>
               </div>
            </div>
         </div>

      </div>
   </div>
</div>

ChatGPT said:

Use Bootstrap row centering and equal column sizing so the last row centers automatically. Since you have 4 members, using col-lg-3 keeps them balanced on large screens, and justify-content-center ensures proper centering on smaller breakpoints.

HTML
<div class="tp-team-area pt-135 pb-100">
   <div class="container">
      <div class="row">
         <div class="col-lg-12">
            <div class="tp-team-2-title-wrap text-center mb-50">

         <!-- Chairperson -->

  • Make them center only when they wrap (tablet/mobile), or

  • Convert to flex card auto-fit layout (cleaner if members increase later), or

  • Add equal height cards + hover elevation effect.


You said:
create an image prompt for an image of the team page
3/3

ChatGPT said:

Here’s a detailed image prompt you can use to generate a visual mockup of your team page:

Prompt:

"Create a professional, modern web page layout for a government commission team. The page should have a clean, light background with a prominent heading at the top: 'Members of The Commission – Electoral Commission Leadership'. Below the heading, display four team member cards in a centered, responsive grid. Each card includes a circular portrait, the member's name, and their title (Chairperson or Commissioner). Include subtle shadow and hover effects on the cards. Add small social media icons (LinkedIn) at the bottom of each card. The design should be elegant, minimal, and visually balanced, with a professional governmental tone. Include realistic portraits for each member and clear typography for the names and titles. The layout should look polished and suitable for a modern institutional website."

If you want, I can also make a shorter AI-friendly version optimized for MidJourney, DALL·E, or Stable Diffusion so it’s ready to generate directly. Do you want me to do that?


You said:
create a prompt for the contact us image

ChatGPT said:

Here’s a detailed prompt for a “Contact Us” page hero image:

Prompt:

"Design a modern and professional 'Contact Us' web page hero image. Include a clean, bright background with soft gradients or abstract shapes. Show a friendly, approachable scene with a team or individual at a desk using a laptop, phone, or headset, symbolizing communication and customer support. Include subtle icons like email envelopes, chat bubbles, and location pins in the background. Use a balanced composition with space for a headline and contact form on the right side. Style should be clean, minimal, and professional, with vibrant but soft colors, clear typography, and a welcoming tone suitable for a corporate or institutional website."

If you want, I can also create a shorter, AI-optimized version that’s ready for MidJourney or DALL·E. Do you want me to do that?


You said:
just an image to suite the commission and not a website design like you gave me

ChatGPT said:

Got it! Here's a prompt tailored for the Electoral Commission team/contact image, more realistic and representative, not a website layout:

Prompt:

"Create a professional group portrait of a diverse leadership team for an electoral commission. Include four members: one chairperson and three commissioners, dressed in formal business attire, standing or seated confidently in a bright, neutral office setting. Show friendly, approachable expressions, with subtle elements representing governance and democracy, such as a national flag, official documents, or a meeting table in the background. Style should be realistic, high-resolution, and polished, suitable for official institutional use."

I can also make a more stylized, illustrative version if you want it to look like a hero image for the team page rather than a photo. Do you want me to create that version too?


You said:
Adapt the contact us form: <div class="col-lg-4 mb-25"> <div class="tp-join-form-wrap tp-join-6-form text-center h-100 tp-bg-grey-3 tp-round-4 h-100"> <h3 class="mb-15 fs-24">Schedule complimentary consultation</h3> <p class="lh-26 tp-text-grey-2 mb-30">Complete the form below, and we’ll schedule a<br> consultation at your convenience.</p> <form action="#"> <input class="tp-input mb-15" type="text" placeholder="Email address*"> <input class="tp-input mb-15" type="text" placeholder="Your location"> <textarea class="tp-input tp-textarea mb-10" placeholder="Write your message"></textarea> <button class="tp-btn-md tp-round-4 fs-16 tp-bg-theme-primary w-100 d-block tp-text-common-white hover-text-white fw-500" type="submit">Submit your require</button> </form> </div> </div>

ChatGPT said:

Here’s an adapted “Contact Us” form version based on your original structure, optimized for clarity, better labels, and a “send message” context:

HTML
<div class="col-lg-4 mb-25">
   <div class="tp-join-form-wrap tp-join-6-form text-center h-100 tp-bg-grey-3 tp-round-4">
      <h3 class="mb-15 fs-24">Contact Us</h3>
      <p class="lh-26 tp-text-grey-2 mb-30">
         Have questions or inquiries? Fill out the form below and we’ll get back to you promptly.
      </p>
      <form action="#" method="post">
         <input class="tp-input mb-15" type="text" name="name" placeholder="Full Name*" required>
         <input class="tp-input mb-15" type="email" name="email" placeholder="Email Address*" required>
         <input class="tp-input mb-15" type="text" name="subject" placeholder="Subject">
         <textarea class="tp-input tp-textarea mb-15" name="message" placeholder="Your Message*" required></textarea>
         <button class="tp-btn-md tp-round-4 fs-16 tp-bg-theme-primary w-100 d-block tp-text-common-white hover-text-white fw-500" type="submit">
            Send Message
         </button>
      </form>
   </div>
</div>

✅ Changes made:

  1. Title changed to “Contact Us”.

  2. Instructions updated to fit general inquiries.

  3. Added Name and Subject fields for better context.

  4. Marked essential fields (Name, Email, Message) as required.

  5. Button text updated to “Send Message”.

  6. Minor cleanup of duplicate h-100 class and spacing for readability.

If you want, I can also style it with icons and a cleaner modern layout like your team cards so it visually matches the rest of the site. Do you want me to do that?

ChatGPT said:

Sure — replace the whole SVG block with a normal logo image inside the same anchor.

✅ Option 1 (recommended): use an <img> logo

HTML
<a href="{{ url('index') }}" class="brand-logo">
  <img
    src="{{ asset('assets/img/logo/ecz-logo.png') }}"
    alt="Electoral Commission of Zambia"
    class="brand-logo-img"
  />
</a>

Add CSS (so it fits where the SVG used to be):

CSS
.brand-logo {
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  gap: 10px;
}

.brand-logo-img{
  height: 55px;      /* match your old icon size */
  width: auto;
  display: block;
  object-fit: contain;
}

✅ Option 2: if you need both icon + text logo (like the SVG had)

HTML
<a href="{{ url('index') }}" class="brand-logo">
  <img src="{{ asset('assets/img/logo/ecz-mark.png') }}" alt="ECZ" class="brand-logo-abbr" />
  <img src="{{ asset('assets/img/logo/ecz-wordmark.png') }}" alt="Electoral Commission of Zambia" class="brand-logo-title" />
</a>
CSS
.brand-logo { display:flex; align-items:center; gap:10px; }
.brand-logo-abbr { height:55px; width:auto; object-fit:contain; }
.brand-logo-title { height:28px; width:auto; object-fit:contain; }

If you tell me your real logo file name/path (or upload it), I’ll plug it in exactly.


You said:
I am using the fillow admin template, and i am failing to make changes to the styling. whenever i change the css file nothing is working

ChatGPT said:

This usually happens in admin templates for one of these reasons: you’re editing the wrong CSS file, the CSS you’re editing is getting overridden, or the browser/server is serving a cached/minified build.

Here’s the fastest way to diagnose + fix it in Fillow (and similar templates):

1) Confirm you’re editing the CSS file that’s actually loaded

Open the page → Right click → Inspect → Network → CSS and refresh.

  • Click the main stylesheet (often something like style.css, custom.css, app.css, vendor.css, main.css)

  • Confirm the URL matches the file you’re editing on disk

  • If the loaded file is something like style.min.css but you’re editing style.css, your changes won’t show.

Quick test: add something extreme at the bottom of the loaded file:

CSS
body { outline: 10px solid red !important; }

If you still don’t see it, you’re not editing the file being served.

2) Template might be using a compiled build (SCSS → CSS)

Many admin templates ship SCSS and compile to /css/style.min.css.

If your page loads a minified file, either:

  • edit the minified file (not ideal), or

  • edit the SCSS source and recompile (ideal)

Check your project for folders like:

  • assets/scss/

  • src/scss/

  • scss/

  • gulpfile.js, webpack.mix.js, vite.config.js, package.json

If it uses build tools, you must rebuild after editing SCSS.

3) Your changes are being overridden (most common)

Even if your CSS loads, template rules might override yours because they load later or are more specific.

Best fix: create a custom override file that loads LAST

Make a file like:

  • assets/css/custom.css

Put overrides there and include it after all other CSS:

HTML
<link rel="stylesheet" href="assets/css/style.css">
<link rel="stylesheet" href="assets/css/custom.css">

Then test with:

CSS
/* custom.css */
.tp-header-4-top { background: #0a2d1c !important; }

4) Browser caching / server caching is hiding changes

Try these:

  • Hard refresh: Ctrl + Shift + R (Windows) / Cmd + Shift + R (Mac)

  • DevTools → Network → ✅ Disable cache → refresh

  • Rename the CSS file query string:

HTML
<link rel="stylesheet" href="assets/css/custom.css?v=2">

If you’re on Laravel / PHP and assets are cached, also run:

Bash
php artisan optimize:clear

5) You’re editing CSS but the template uses inline styles or JS theme switcher

Some templates dynamically inject theme colors with JS or CSS variables.

Check for:

  • :root { --primary: ... }

  • files like theme.js, settings.js, styleSwitcher.js

  • a “Theme Settings” panel that stores values in localStorage

If the theme switcher is active, it can override your CSS every refresh.

6) If this is Laravel Blade: make sure you aren’t editing in the wrong place

Sometimes the template has:

  • public/assets/css/... (used by browser)

  • resources/assets/... or resources/css/... (source)

If the browser loads from public/, edit the file in public/ or rebuild from the source.

Quick fix that works 90% of the time

  1. Create public/assets/css/custom.css

  2. Include it last in your layout

  3. Put all your overrides there with !important only when needed

If you paste the <link rel="stylesheet"...> lines from your template header (or screenshot the Network → CSS list), I’ll tell you the exact file Fillow is actually using and where to place your overrides.


You said:
Create more realistic image prompts for the hero section. People should be happy
2/2

ChatGPT said:

Got it — here are more realistic, documentary-style hero image prompts with happy, natural people (not staged, not stock-photo vibes). These are tuned for wide hero banners and civic authenticity:

🇿🇲 Prompt 1 — Joyful First-Time Voters

Modern young Zambian adults smiling and chatting while lining up at a Lusaka polling station, some holding voter cards casually, relaxed body language, genuine laughter, colorful everyday clothing, soft morning sunlight filtering through open windows, candid human moments, documentary realism.

Natural African daylight color grading, ultra-realistic skin tones and textures, shallow depth of field, civic environment with posters and ballot signage in background, high-end photojournalism style, wide cinematic hero banner composition.

🇿🇲 Prompt 2 — Community Pride

Mixed-age Zambian citizens standing together outside a polling station, elders and youth smiling warmly, friendly conversations, children nearby, subtle Zambia flags in background, bright midday sun, authentic African urban setting, joyful community atmosphere.

Photorealistic documentary photography, natural expressions, soft contrast, realistic textures, wide hero layout, civic pride mood, environmental storytelling.

🇿🇲 Prompt 3 — Confident Young Professionals

Happy young Zambian professionals arriving at a polling station, dressed in smart-casual clothing, confident smiles, shaking hands and greeting friends, holding voter cards, Lusaka city background, clean modern civic space.

Ultra-realistic lighting, cinematic depth of field, warm color grading, human-focused realism, editorial photography style, wide hero banner framing.

🇿🇲 Prompt 4 — Women Leading Participation

Smiling Zambian women in colorful chitenge and modern outfits queuing at a polling station, joyful expressions, friendly conversations, sunlight highlighting natural skin tones, empowering civic moment, candid composition.

High-end documentary realism, soft highlights, realistic shadows, shallow DOF, inclusive African representation, wide website hero format.

🇿🇲 Prompt 5 — Youth Energy & Optimism

Energetic Zambian youth laughing together while waiting to vote, relaxed posture, phones in pockets, voter cards visible, modern Lusaka polling station interior, sunlight streaming through glass doors, spontaneous joyful moments.

Ultra-photorealistic textures, natural African daylight, cinematic realism, lifestyle documentary photography, large panoramic hero composition.

🇿🇲 Prompt 6 — Family Voting Day

Happy Zambian family arriving at polling station, parents smiling, young adults beside them, warm greetings with election officials, bright daylight, authentic civic setting, hopeful atmosphere.

Editorial photojournalism style, realistic color grading, soft background blur, emotional storytelling, wide hero banner crop.

If you’d like, next I can also give you:

✅ versions with green ECZ gradient overlay baked into the prompt
✅ versions optimized for Midjourney / SDXL / Leonardo / Grok
✅ prompts with urban Lusaka vs suburban polling stations
✅ prompts emphasizing trust + transparency + national unity

Just tell me 👍


You said:
give me something different

ChatGPT said:

Alright — let’s switch it up completely. Here are fresh, different hero image directions that still feel real, joyful, and civic, but avoid the “queue-at-a-polling-station” look you’ve already used.

🌍 Direction 1: After Voting (Relief & Pride)

Zambian citizens walking away from a polling station after voting, smiling with visible relief and pride, some showing inked fingers subtly, relaxed body language, casual conversations, late-afternoon golden light, authentic Lusaka neighborhood setting.

Photojournalistic realism, warm golden-hour tones, natural African skin textures, shallow depth of field, candid storytelling, wide cinematic hero banner.

🌱 Direction 2: Civic Trust & Calm

Happy Zambian election officials interacting warmly with citizens inside a calm, well-organized polling station, friendly smiles, respectful gestures, transparent ballot boxes visible, bright natural daylight, clean and orderly civic environment.

Ultra-realistic documentary photography, neutral color grading, soft highlights, trust-focused atmosphere, wide hero composition.

🤝 Direction 3: Unity Without Queues

Diverse Zambian citizens standing together in small groups outside a civic building, smiling and chatting confidently, no visible lines, relaxed atmosphere, subtle national colors in clothing, trees and open sky in background.

Natural daylight photography, authentic African urban textures, candid human interaction, optimistic civic mood, panoramic hero framing.

🗳️ Direction 4: The Moment of Participation

Close-up documentary shot of a smiling Zambian voter placing a ballot into a transparent ballot box, hands in focus, face softly blurred in background, sunlight illuminating the scene, symbolic yet realistic civic moment.

Ultra-realistic textures, shallow depth of field, cinematic focus, editorial photojournalism style, wide hero-safe composition.

☀️ Direction 5: Morning of Democracy

Early morning in Lusaka as citizens arrive at a polling station, fresh light, soft smiles, people greeting each other warmly, calm and hopeful atmosphere, dew-lit surroundings, peaceful civic energy.

Natural African morning light, realistic color grading, soft contrast, documentary realism, wide hero banner crop.

🌐 Direction 6: Democracy Beyond the Polling Station

Happy Zambian citizens discussing civic matters in a public space—outside a community hall or under trees—smiling, engaged, relaxed postures, everyday life continuing around them.

Lifestyle documentary photography, realistic African environment, human-centered storytelling, warm and optimistic tone, wide hero layout.

If you want, next we can:

  • Go symbolic (hands, flags, light, movement)

  • Go cinematic editorial (very shallow DOF, dramatic light)

  • Go quiet + powerful (minimal people, strong emotion)

  • Add ECZ green gradient naturally into the lighting

  • Make prompts less literal, more emotional

Just tell me which direction you want to lean into next.

<div class="tp-project-area tp-project-2-animate-tab pt-135 pb-110">
  <div class="container">

      <div class="col-lg-12">

          <span class="fw-500 fs-16 tp-text-grey-1 mb-10 d-inline-block">Political parties in Zambia</span>
          <h2 class="mb-20 fs-xl-40 fs-sm-36 wow img-custom-anim-left" data-wow-duration="1.5s" data-wow-delay="0.2s">

              <div class="row">
                <!-- Socialist Party -->
                <div class="col-xl-4 col-lg-6 col-md-6 mb-30">

                      <img class="w-100 tp-round-4" src="assets/img/parties/socialist.jpg" alt="Socialist Party">

You can find your assigned station on our official website or via SMS notifications once registered.</p> </div> </div> </div> <!-- Question 3 --> <div class="accordion-item"> <h2 class="accordion-header" id="faq_three"> <button style="color:black;" class="accordion-button faq-expend collapsed tp-faq-btn-2" type="button" data-bs-toggle="collapse" data-bs-target="#faq__collapse_three" aria-expanded="false" aria-controls="faq__collapse_three"> <span>03</span> How are election results verified? </button> </h2> <div id="faq__collapse_three" class="accordion-collapse collapse" aria-labelledby="faq_three" data-bs-parent="#commission_faqaccordion"> <div class="accordion-body tp-faq-details-para"> <p>Results are verified through a multi-tier system involving polling station reports, constituency-level tabulations, and independent audits to ensure accuracy and transparency.</p> </div> </div> </div> <!-- Question 4 --> <div class="accordion-item"> <h2 class="accordion-header" id="faq_four"> <button style="color:black;" class="accordion-button faq-expend collapsed tp-faq-btn-2" type="button" data-bs-toggle="collapse" data-bs-target="#faq__collapse_four" aria-expanded="false" aria-controls="faq__collapse_four"> <span>04</span> What is the role of the commission? </button> </h2> <div id="faq__collapse_four" class="accordion-collapse collapse" aria-labelledby="faq_four" data-bs-parent="#commission_faqaccordion"> <div class="accordion-body tp-faq-details-para"> <p>The commission oversees all aspects of elections, including voter registration, polling station management, election monitoring, and announcing official results in accordance with national laws.</p> </div> </div> </div> </div> </div> </div> </div> </div> </div>

ChatGPT said:
HTML
<!-- FAQ Section (your existing code) -->
<div class="tp-faq-area pt-140 pb-110">
  <div class="container">
    <div class="row">
      <!-- FAQ Image -->
      <div class="col-lg-6">
        <div class="tp-faq-4-thumb p-relative z-index-1 mb-30">
          <span class="tp-faq-4-thumb-shape p-absolute"></span>
          <div class="ml-30 thumb mr-85">
            <img class="w-100 tp-round-4" src="assets/img/faq/faq-4/thumb.jpg" alt="Commissions FAQ">
          </div>
        </div>
      </div>

      <!-- FAQ Accordion -->
      <div class="col-lg-6">
        <div class="tp-faq-wrapper tp-faq-4-wrapper mb-30">
          <h2 class="mb-20 fs-40 lh-1 fs-sm-36 wow img-custom-anim-right" data-wow-duration="1.5s" data-wow-delay="0.2s">
            Learn about <span class="text-italic fw-400 tp-ff-heading">our election processes.</span>
          </h2>
          <p class="mb-40">
            Here we answer the most common questions about voter registration, polling stations, and commission operations.
          </p>

          <div class="accordion" id="commission_faqaccordion">
            <!-- Question 1 -->
            <div class="accordion-item expand">
              <h2 style="color:black;" class="accordion-header" id="faq_one">
                <button style="color:black;" class="accordion-button faq-expend tp-faq-btn-2" type="button" data-bs-toggle="collapse"
                  data-bs-target="#faq__collapse_one" aria-expanded="true" aria-controls="faq__collapse_one">
                  <span>01</span>
                  How do I register to vote?
                </button>
              </h2>
              <div id="faq__collapse_one" class="accordion-collapse collapse show" aria-labelledby="faq_one"
                data-bs-parent="#commission_faqaccordion">
                <div class="accordion-body tp-faq-details-para">
                  <p>You can register online or at your local registration office. Ensure you have valid identification and proof of residence to complete the registration process.</p>
                </div>
              </div>
            </div>

            <!-- Question 2 -->
            <div class="accordion-item">
              <h2 style="color:black;" class="accordion-header" id="faq_two">
                <button style="color:black;" class="accordion-button faq-expend collapsed tp-faq-btn-2" type="button"
                  data-bs-toggle="collapse" data-bs-target="#faq__collapse_two" aria-expanded="false" aria-controls="faq__collapse_two">
                  <span>02</span>
                  Where are the polling stations located?
                </button>
              </h2>
              <div id="faq__collapse_two" class="accordion-collapse collapse" aria-labelledby="faq_two"
                data-bs-parent="#commission_faqaccordion">
                <div class="accordion-body tp-faq-details-para">
                  <p>Polling stations are located across all districts and wards. You can find your assigned station on our official website or via SMS notifications once registered.</p>
                </div>
              </div>
            </div>

            <!-- Question 3 -->
            <div class="accordion-item">
              <h2 class="accordion-header" id="faq_three">
                <button style="color:black;" class="accordion-button faq-expend collapsed tp-faq-btn-2" type="button"
                  data-bs-toggle="collapse" data-bs-target="#faq__collapse_three" aria-expanded="false" aria-controls="faq__collapse_three">
                  <span>03</span>
                  How are election results verified?
                </button>
              </h2>
              <div id="faq__collapse_three" class="accordion-collapse collapse" aria-labelledby="faq_three"
                data-bs-parent="#commission_faqaccordion">
                <div class="accordion-body tp-faq-details-para">
                  <p>Results are verified through a multi-tier system involving polling station reports, constituency-level tabulations, and independent audits to ensure accuracy and transparency.</p>
                </div>
              </div>
            </div>

            <!-- Question 4 -->
            <div class="accordion-item">
              <h2 class="accordion-header" id="faq_four">
                <button style="color:black;" class="accordion-button faq-expend collapsed tp-faq-btn-2" type="button"
                  data-bs-toggle="collapse" data-bs-target="#faq__collapse_four" aria-expanded="false" aria-controls="faq__collapse_four">
                  <span>04</span>
                  What is the role of the commission?
                </button>
              </h2>
              <div id="faq__collapse_four" class="accordion-collapse collapse" aria-labelledby="faq_four"
                data-bs-parent="#commission_faqaccordion">
                <div class="accordion-body tp-faq-details-para">
                  <p>The commission oversees all aspects of elections, including voter registration, polling station management, election monitoring, and announcing official results in accordance with national laws.</p>
                </div>
              </div>
            </div>
          </div><!-- /accordion -->
        </div>
      </div>
    </div>
  </div>
</div>
<!-- /FAQ Section -->

<!-- COUNTDOWN SECTION (add after FAQ) -->
<section class="tp-countdown-area pt-80 pb-80 tp-bg-grey-3">
  <div class="container">
    <div class="row align-items-center">
      <div class="col-lg-7 mb-30">
        <div class="tp-countdown-content">
          <span class="fw-500 fs-16 tp-text-grey-1 mb-10 d-inline-block">Important dates</span>
          <h2 class="mb-15 fs-40 fs-sm-32">
            Countdown to <span class="text-italic fw-400 tp-ff-heading">Election Day</span>
          </h2>
          <p class="tp-text-grey-2 mb-0">
            Track time remaining to the next key electoral milestone. This countdown will automatically update.
          </p>
        </div>
      </div>

      <div class="col-lg-5 mb-30">
        <div class="tp-countdown-wrap tp-round-4 tp-bg-common-white p-30 text-center">
          <div class="tp-countdown-grid d-flex align-items-center justify-content-between gap-10" id="electionCountdown">
            <div class="tp-countdown-item">
              <h3 class="count-num mb-0" data-days>00</h3>
              <span class="tp-text-grey-1 fs-14">Days</span>
            </div>
            <div class="tp-countdown-item">
              <h3 class="count-num mb-0" data-hours>00</h3>
              <span class="tp-text-grey-1 fs-14">Hours</span>
            </div>
            <div class="tp-countdown-item">
              <h3 class="count-num mb-0" data-minutes>00</h3>
              <span class="tp-text-grey-1 fs-14">Minutes</span>
            </div>
            <div class="tp-countdown-item">
              <h3 class="count-num mb-0" data-seconds>00</h3>
              <span class="tp-text-grey-1 fs-14">Seconds</span>
            </div>
          </div>

          <div class="mt-20">
            <small class="tp-text-grey-2 d-block">
              Target date: <span id="countdownTargetLabel">2026-08-13 00:00</span>
            </small>
            <small class="tp-text-grey-2 d-block" id="countdownStatus"></small>
          </div>
        </div>
      </div>
    </div>
  </div>
</section>

<!-- Optional tiny CSS (put in your stylesheet) -->
<style>
  .tp-countdown-wrap { box-shadow: 0 10px 30px rgba(0,0,0,.06); }
  .tp-countdown-item { width: 24%; }
  .tp-countdown-item .count-num { font-size: 34px; line-height: 1; }
  @media (max-width: 575px) {
    .tp-countdown-grid { flex-wrap: wrap; }
    .tp-countdown-item { width: 48%; padding: 10px 0; }
  }
</style>

<!-- Countdown JS (put before </body>) -->
<script>
  (function () {
    /**
     * ✅ Set your target date/time here
     * Format: "YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS" (local time)
     * Example below: 13 Aug 2026 00:00:00
     */
    const TARGET = "2026-08-13T00:00:00";

    const root = document.getElementById("electionCountdown");
    if (!root) return;

    const elDays = root.querySelector("[data-days]");
    const elHours = root.querySelector("[data-hours]");
    const elMinutes = root.querySelector("[data-minutes]");
    const elSeconds = root.querySelector("[data-seconds]");

    const label = document.getElementById("countdownTargetLabel");
    const status = document.getElementById("countdownStatus");

    const targetDate = new Date(TARGET);
    if (label) {
      // Keep label simple + readable
      const pad = (n) => String(n).padStart(2, "0");
      label.textContent = `${targetDate.getFullYear()}-${pad(targetDate.getMonth() + 1)}-${pad(targetDate.getDate())} ${pad(targetDate.getHours())}:${pad(targetDate.getMinutes())}`;
    }

    const pad2 = (n) => String(n).padStart(2, "0");

    function tick() {
      const now = new Date();
      const diff = targetDate.getTime() - now.getTime();

      if (diff <= 0) {
        elDays.textContent = "00";
        elHours.textContent = "00";
        elMinutes.textContent = "00";
        elSeconds.textContent = "00";
        if (status) status.textContent = "The countdown has ended.";
        return;
      }

      const totalSeconds = Math.floor(diff / 1000);
      const days = Math.floor(totalSeconds / (60 * 60 * 24));
      const hours = Math.floor((totalSeconds % (60 * 60 * 24)) / (60 * 60));
      const minutes = Math.floor((totalSeconds % (60 * 60)) / 60);
      const seconds = totalSeconds % 60;

      elDays.textContent = pad2(days);
      elHours.textContent = pad2(hours);
      elMinutes.textContent = pad2(minutes);
      elSeconds.textContent = pad2(seconds);

      if (status) status.textContent = "";
    }

    tick();
    setInterval(tick, 1000);
  })();
</script>

You said:
Design it better an the colors of the times should have : #f17121, #0d9153 #ec1c24 and black

ChatGPT said:
HTML
<!-- COUNTDOWN SECTION (Improved design + colored time boxes) -->
<section class="tp-countdown-area pt-90 pb-90 tp-countdown-modern">
  <div class="container">
    <div class="row align-items-center">
      <div class="col-lg-7 mb-30">
        <div class="tp-countdown-content">
          <span class="fw-500 fs-16 tp-text-grey-1 mb-10 d-inline-block">Important dates</span>
          <h2 class="mb-15 fs-40 fs-sm-32">
            Countdown to <span class="text-italic fw-400 tp-ff-heading">Election Day</span>
          </h2>
          <p class="tp-text-grey-2 mb-0">
            This timer updates every second and helps voters track the next key milestone.
          </p>
        </div>
      </div>

      <div class="col-lg-5 mb-30">
        <div class="tp-countdown-card tp-round-4">
          <div class="tp-countdown-head">
            <div class="tp-countdown-badge">Live Countdown</div>
            <div class="tp-countdown-meta">
              Target: <span id="countdownTargetLabel">2026-08-13 00:00</span>
            </div>
          </div>

          <div class="tp-countdown-grid" id="electionCountdown">
            <!-- Days -->
            <div class="tp-timebox is-days">
              <div class="tp-timebox-top">
                <span class="tp-dot"></span>
                <span class="tp-label">Days</span>
              </div>
              <div class="tp-timebox-num" data-days>00</div>
            </div>

            <!-- Hours -->
            <div class="tp-timebox is-hours">
              <div class="tp-timebox-top">
                <span class="tp-dot"></span>
                <span class="tp-label">Hours</span>
              </div>
              <div class="tp-timebox-num" data-hours>00</div>
            </div>

            <!-- Minutes -->
            <div class="tp-timebox is-minutes">
              <div class="tp-timebox-top">
                <span class="tp-dot"></span>
                <span class="tp-label">Minutes</span>
              </div>
              <div class="tp-timebox-num" data-minutes>00</div>
            </div>

            <!-- Seconds -->
            <div class="tp-timebox is-seconds">
              <div class="tp-timebox-top">
                <span class="tp-dot"></span>
                <span class="tp-label">Seconds</span>
              </div>
              <div class="tp-timebox-num" data-seconds>00</div>
            </div>
          </div>

          <div class="tp-countdown-foot">
            <small class="tp-text-grey-2" id="countdownStatus"></small>
          </div>
        </div>
      </div>
    </div>
  </div>
</section>

<!-- BETTER STYLES (place in your stylesheet) -->
<style>
  .tp-countdown-modern{
    background: linear-gradient(180deg, rgba(13,145,83,.06) 0%, rgba(241,113,33,.06) 55%, rgba(236,28,36,.05) 100%);
  }

  .tp-countdown-card{
    background:#fff;
    padding: 22px 22px 18px;
    box-shadow: 0 18px 45px rgba(0,0,0,.10);
    border: 1px solid rgba(0,0,0,.06);
    position: relative;
    overflow: hidden;
  }

  /* subtle accent stripe */
  .tp-countdown-card:before{
    content:"";
    position:absolute;
    inset: 0;
    height: 6px;
    background: linear-gradient(90deg, #f17121 0%, #0d9153 35%, #ec1c24 70%, #000 100%);
  }

  .tp-countdown-head{
    display:flex;
    align-items:center;
    justify-content:space-between;
    gap: 12px;
    padding-top: 8px;
    margin-bottom: 16px;
  }

  .tp-countdown-badge{
    font-size: 12px;
    font-weight: 700;
    letter-spacing: .2px;
    padding: 6px 10px;
    border-radius: 999px;
    color:#111;
    background: rgba(0,0,0,.05);
  }

  .tp-countdown-meta{
    font-size: 12px;
    color: rgba(0,0,0,.55);
    white-space: nowrap;
  }

  .tp-countdown-grid{
    display:grid;
    grid-template-columns: repeat(4, 1fr);
    gap: 12px;
  }

  .tp-timebox{
    border-radius: 14px;
    padding: 12px 12px 14px;
    color:#fff;
    position: relative;
    overflow:hidden;
    transform: translateZ(0);
    box-shadow: 0 12px 22px rgba(0,0,0,.12);
  }

  /* glass highlight */
  .tp-timebox:before{
    content:"";
    position:absolute;
    top:-40%;
    left:-30%;
    width: 140%;
    height: 120%;
    background: radial-gradient(circle at 30% 30%, rgba(255,255,255,.30) 0%, rgba(255,255,255,0) 55%);
    pointer-events:none;
  }

  .tp-timebox-top{
    display:flex;
    align-items:center;
    justify-content:space-between;
    margin-bottom: 10px;
  }

  .tp-dot{
    width: 8px;
    height: 8px;
    border-radius: 99px;
    background: rgba(255,255,255,.9);
    box-shadow: 0 0 0 4px rgba(255,255,255,.18);
  }

  .tp-label{
    font-size: 12px;
    letter-spacing: .6px;
    text-transform: uppercase;
    opacity: .92;
    font-weight: 600;
  }

  .tp-timebox-num{
    font-size: 36px;
    line-height: 1;
    font-weight: 800;
    letter-spacing: 1px;
  }

  /* REQUIRED COLORS */
  .tp-timebox.is-days{
    background: linear-gradient(135deg, #f17121 0%, #d95e17 100%);
  }
  .tp-timebox.is-hours{
    background: linear-gradient(135deg, #0d9153 0%, #0a7543 100%);
  }
  .tp-timebox.is-minutes{
    background: linear-gradient(135deg, #ec1c24 0%, #c5141a 100%);
  }
  .tp-timebox.is-seconds{
    background: linear-gradient(135deg, #000 0%, #2b2b2b 100%);
  }

  .tp-countdown-foot{
    margin-top: 14px;
    padding-top: 12px;
    border-top: 1px dashed rgba(0,0,0,.12);
    text-align: center;
  }

  /* responsive */
  @media (max-width: 575px){
    .tp-countdown-grid{ grid-template-columns: repeat(2, 1fr); }
    .tp-timebox-num{ font-size: 32px; }
  }
</style>

<!-- Countdown JS (same logic, keep before </body>) -->
<script>
  (function () {
    // ✅ Change the date to your real election date/time
    const TARGET = "2026-08-13T00:00:00"; // local time

    const root = document.getElementById("electionCountdown");
    if (!root) return;

    const elDays = root.querySelector("[data-days]");
    const elHours = root.querySelector("[data-hours]");
    const elMinutes = root.querySelector("[data-minutes]");
    const elSeconds = root.querySelector("[data-seconds]");

    const label = document.getElementById("countdownTargetLabel");
    const status = document.getElementById("countdownStatus");

    const targetDate = new Date(TARGET);

    const pad2 = (n) => String(n).padStart(2, "0");
    const pad = (n) => String(n).padStart(2, "0");

    if (label) {
      label.textContent = `${targetDate.getFullYear()}-${pad(targetDate.getMonth() + 1)}-${pad(targetDate.getDate())} ${pad(targetDate.getHours())}:${pad(targetDate.getMinutes())}`;
    }

    function tick() {
      const now = new Date();
      const diff = targetDate.getTime() - now.getTime();

      if (diff <= 0) {
        elDays.textContent = "00";
        elHours.textContent = "00";
        elMinutes.textContent = "00";
        elSeconds.textContent = "00";
        if (status) status.textContent = "The countdown has ended.";
        return;
      }

      const totalSeconds = Math.floor(diff / 1000);
      const days = Math.floor(totalSeconds / (60 * 60 * 24));
      const hours = Math.floor((totalSeconds % (60 * 60 * 24)) / (60 * 60));
      const minutes = Math.floor((totalSeconds % (60 * 60)) / 60);
      const seconds = totalSeconds % 60;

      elDays.textContent = pad2(days);
      elHours.textContent = pad2(hours);
      elMinutes.textContent = pad2(minutes);
      elSeconds.textContent = pad2(seconds);

      if (status) status.textContent = "";
    }

    tick();
    setInterval(tick, 1000);
  })();
</script>

🎨 Color Code Details

background-color: #db5012;
border-color: #db5012;

You said:
How can i add a polling station location on a map?

ChatGPT said:

You’ve got a few solid options, depending on how interactive you want the map to be and how many polling stations you’re showing. I’ll break it down from simple → advanced, with copy-paste examples.

✅ OPTION 1: Simple & Fast — Google Maps Embed (Best for single locations)

Perfect if you just want to show one polling station clearly.

Steps

  1. Open Google Maps

  2. Search the polling station (or drop a pin)

  3. Click Share → Embed a map

  4. Copy the <iframe>

Example (drop straight into your page)

HTML
<div class="tp-map-wrap tp-round-4 overflow-hidden">
  <iframe
    src="https://www.google.com/maps/embed?pb=!1m18!1m12!1m3!1d3846.2220703749836!2d28.3084131!3d-15.4185611"
    width="100%"
    height="450"
    style="border:0;"
    allowfullscreen=""
    loading="lazy"
    referrerpolicy="no-referrer-when-downgrade">
  </iframe>
</div>

✔ No API key
✔ Very reliable
❌ Not dynamic (no filtering/search)

✅ OPTION 2: Multiple Polling Stations — Google Maps JavaScript API

Best if you want:

  • Multiple locations

  • Custom markers

  • Search / filters

  • District-based grouping

1️⃣ Load Google Maps API

HTML
<script src="https://maps.googleapis.com/maps/api/js?key=YOUR_API_KEY"></script>

2️⃣ HTML container

HTML
<div id="pollingMap" style="height:500px;" class="tp-round-4"></div>

3️⃣ JavaScript (example)

HTML
<script>
function initMap() {
  const map = new google.maps.Map(document.getElementById("pollingMap"), {
    zoom: 12,
    center: { lat: -15.4167, lng: 28.2833 } // Lusaka
  });

  const pollingStations = [
    {
      name: "Civic Centre Polling Station",
      position: { lat: -15.4167, lng: 28.2833 }
    },
    {
      name: "University of Zambia",
      position: { lat: -15.3916, lng: 28.3345 }
    }
  ];

  pollingStations.forEach(station => {
    new google.maps.Marker({
      position: station.position,
      map,
      title: station.name
    });
  });
}

window.onload = initMap;
</script>

✔ Scales well
✔ Interactive
✔ Professional
❌ Requires API key

✅ OPTION 3: OpenStreetMap + Leaflet (No Google, No API Key)

This is excellent for public institutions like an electoral commission.

1️⃣ Load Leaflet

HTML
<link rel="stylesheet" href="https://unpkg.com/leaflet/dist/leaflet.css" />
<script src="https://unpkg.com/leaflet/dist/leaflet.js"></script>

2️⃣ Map container

HTML
<div id="pollingMap" style="height:500px;" class="tp-round-4"></div>

3️⃣ JavaScript

HTML
<script>
const map = L.map('pollingMap').setView([-15.4167, 28.2833], 12);

L.tileLayer('https://{s}.tile.openstreetmap.org/{z}/{x}/{y}.png', {
  attribution: '&copy; OpenStreetMap contributors'
}).addTo(map);

L.marker([-15.4167, 28.2833])
  .addTo(map)
  .bindPopup('<strong>Civic Centre Polling Station</strong><br>Lusaka')
  .openPopup();
</script>

✔ Free
✔ Transparent
✔ Government-friendly
✔ Highly customizable

🎯 Recommended for ECZ-type Website

Use CaseBest Option
Single polling stationGoogle Embed
Many polling stationsLeaflet + OpenStreetMap
District / ward filteringGoogle Maps API or Leaflet
Public trust & neutralityLeaflet + OSM

💡 UX Enhancements You Can Add

  • 🔍 Search by District / Ward

  • 📍 “Find my polling station” (GPS)

  • 📱 Mobile-friendly full-width map

  • 🎨 ECZ-colored custom markers

  • 🕒 Show polling hours in popup

If you want, next I can:

  • Build a searchable polling station map

  • Connect map to a database / JSON

  • Style markers with ECZ colors

  • Add countdown + map combo section

Just say the word 👌

✅ Updated FAQ Block (Election Phase Version)

HTML
<div class="col-lg-6">
    <div class="tp-faq-wrapper tp-faq-4-wrapper mb-30">
        <h2 class="mb-20 fs-40 lh-1 fs-sm-36 wow img-custom-anim-right" data-wow-duration="1.5s" data-wow-delay="0.2s">
            Prepare for <span class="text-italic fw-400 tp-ff-heading">Election Day.</span>
        </h2>
        <p class="mb-40">
            Find essential information about voting procedures, polling stations, and how results are managed as we approach the general elections.

        <div class="accordion" id="commission_faqaccordion">

            <!-- Question 1 -->
            <div class="accordion-item expand">
                <h2 class="accordion-header" id="faq_one">
                    <button style="color:black;" class="accordion-button faq-expend tp-faq-btn-2" type="button" data-bs-toggle="collapse" data-bs-target="#faq__collapse_one" aria-expanded="true">
                        <span>01</span>
                        What should I bring on Election Day?
                    </button>
                </h2>
                <div id="faq__collapse_one" class="accordion-collapse collapse show">
                    <div class="accordion-body tp-faq-details-para">
                        <p>Please bring your NRC or valid voter identification. Voters are encouraged to arrive early and follow all instructions provided by polling officials.</p>
                    </div>
                </div>
            </div>

            <!-- Question 2 -->
            <div class="accordion-item">
                <h2 class="accordion-header" id="faq_two">
                    <button style="color:black;" class="accordion-button faq-expend collapsed tp-faq-btn-2" type="button" data-bs-toggle="collapse" data-bs-target="#faq__collapse_two">
                        <span>02</span>
                        How do I find my assigned polling station?
                    </button>
                </h2>
                <div id="faq__collapse_two" class="accordion-collapse collapse">
                    <div class="accordion-body tp-faq-details-para">
                        <p>Your polling station can be confirmed via the official ECZ website, SMS services, or district offices. Voters must vote at their designated stations.</p>
                    </div>
                </div>
            </div>

            <!-- Question 3 -->
            <div class="accordion-item">
                <h2 class="accordion-header" id="faq_three">
                    <button style="color:black;" class="accordion-button faq-expend collapsed tp-faq-btn-2" type="button" data-bs-toggle="collapse" data-bs-target="#faq__collapse_three">
                        <span>03</span>
                        How does the voting process work?
                    </button>
                </h2>
                <div id="faq__collapse_three" class="accordion-collapse collapse">
                    <div class="accordion-body tp-faq-details-para">
                        <p>After verification, voters receive ballot papers, vote in private, and deposit ballots into sealed boxes under the supervision of election officials and observers.</p>
                    </div>
                </div>
            </div>

            <!-- Question 4 -->
            <div class="accordion-item">
                <h2 class="accordion-header" id="faq_four">
                    <button style="color:black;" class="accordion-button faq-expend collapsed tp-faq-btn-2" type="button" data-bs-toggle="collapse" data-bs-target="#faq__collapse_four">
                        <span>04</span>
                        How are results counted and announced?
                    </button>
                </h2>
                <div id="faq__collapse_four" class="accordion-collapse collapse">
                    <div class="accordion-body tp-faq-details-para">
                        <p>Votes are counted at polling stations, verified at constituency level, and officially announced by the Commission following established legal procedures.</p>
                    </div>
                </div>
            </div>

            <!-- Question 5 -->
            <div class="accordion-item">
                <h2 class="accordion-header" id="faq_five">
                    <button style="color:black;" class="accordion-button faq-expend collapsed tp-faq-btn-2" type="button" data-bs-toggle="collapse" data-bs-target="#faq__collapse_five">
                        <span>05</span>
                        What is the role of the Electoral Commission during elections?
                    </button>
                </h2>
                <div id="faq__collapse_five" class="accordion-collapse collapse">
                    <div class="accordion-body tp-faq-details-para">
                        <p>The Commission manages polling operations, deploys election materials, accredits observers, ensures transparency, and declares official results.</p>
                    </div>
                </div>
            </div>

        </div>
    </div>
</div>

✅ What Changed

• Removed voter registration
• Focused on Election Day readiness
• Added voting process clarity
• Emphasized transparency & results flow
• Strengthened civic tone

If you’d like next, I can also add:

✅ “Know Before You Vote” checklist

✅ Security & observers section

Just tell me 👍


You said:
What does this do? .loading { width: 60px; aspect-ratio: 2; --_g: no-repeat radial-gradient(circle closest-side,#f8d0b3 90%,#0000); background: var(--_g) 0% 50%, var(--_g) 50% 50%, var(--_g) 100% 50%; background-size: 33.3333333333% 50%; animation: l3 1s infinite linear; }

ChatGPT said:

Good question — this is a pure-CSS loading animation, no images, no JS. Let’s break it down in plain terms.

What this .loading class does (visually)

It creates a three-dot loading indicator:

● ● ●
that pulse/move horizontally in a loop — the classic “loading…” animation you see while content is fetching.

Step-by-step explanation

1️⃣ The container size

CSS
width: 60px;
aspect-ratio: 2;

  • width: 60px → total width of the loader

  • aspect-ratio: 2 → height becomes 30px

  • Keeps the loader responsive without hard-coding height

2️⃣ The dot shape (magic part)

CSS
--_g: no-repeat radial-gradient(circle closest-side,#f8d0b3 90%,#0000);

This defines a CSS variable containing:

  • A radial gradient shaped like a circle

  • Color: #f8d0b3

  • closest-side ensures perfect circle

  • 90% filled, then transparent (#0000)

👉 This gradient = one dot

3️⃣ Creating three dots

CSS
background:
  var(--_g) 0% 50%,
  var(--_g) 50% 50%,
  var(--_g) 100% 50%;

  • Reuses the same dot three times

  • Positions them:

    • Left (0%)

    • Center (50%)

    • Right (100%)

  • Vertically centered at 50%

4️⃣ Dot sizing

CSS
background-size: 33.3333333333% 50%;

Each dot:

  • Takes 1/3 of the width

  • Half the container height

5️⃣ Animation hook

CSS
animation: l3 1s infinite linear;

This applies a keyframe animation called l3:

  • Duration: 1s

  • Infinite loop

  • Constant speed

⚠️ Important:
This code won’t animate unless @keyframes l3 exists somewhere else in your CSS.

Typical @keyframes l3 (what’s missing)

Usually it looks like this:

CSS
@keyframes l3 {
  20% { background-position: 0% 50%, 50% 50%, 100% 50%; }
  40% { background-position: 0% 50%, 50% 50%, 100% 50%; }
  60% { background-position: 50% 50%, 100% 50%, 0% 50%; }
  80% { background-position: 100% 50%, 0% 50%, 50% 50%; }
}

This creates the sliding / pulsing effect.

Where this is typically used

✅ Page loading screens
✅ AJAX / API fetch indicators
✅ Form submission feedback
✅ Dashboard skeleton loaders

Example HTML:

HTML
<div class="loading"></div>

Want it in ECZ colors?

You can instantly adapt it:

CSS
--_g: no-repeat radial-gradient(circle closest-side,#0d9153 90%,#0000);

Or rotate colors per dot if you want a national-color animation 🇿🇲.

If you want, I can:

  • Rebuild it as a countdown loader

  • Sync it with your election countdown

  • Convert it to SVG for sharper rendering

Just say the word.


You said:
I want the three dots to be in the #0c9154, #ed1c24 and #f37221. the background should also be white

ChatGPT said:

Got it — you want:

✅ Three dots in different colors

  • Green → #0c9154

  • Red → #ed1c24

  • Orange → #f37221

✅ White background
✅ Same animated 3-dot loader

Because your current version uses one gradient reused 3 times, we must split it into three separate gradients (one per dot).

Here’s the clean working version 👇

✅ Final Loader (ECZ Colors + White Background)

CSS
.loading {
  width: 60px;
  aspect-ratio: 2;
  background-color: #ffffff;

  background:
    no-repeat radial-gradient(circle closest-side, #0c9154 90%, transparent) 0% 50%,
    no-repeat radial-gradient(circle closest-side, #ed1c24 90%, transparent) 50% 50%,
    no-repeat radial-gradient(circle closest-side, #f37221 90%, transparent) 100% 50%;

  background-size: 33.333% 50%;
  animation: dots 1s infinite linear;
}

/* Animation */
@keyframes dots {
  0%   { background-position: 0% 50%, 50% 50%, 100% 50%; }
  33%  { background-position: 50% 50%, 100% 50%, 0% 50%; }
  66%  { background-position: 100% 50%, 0% 50%, 50% 50%; }
  100% { background-position: 0% 50%, 50% 50%, 100% 50%; }
}

✅ HTML

HTML
<div class="loading"></div>

What this gives you

🟢 Green dot
🔴 Red dot
🟠 Orange dot

They rotate smoothly in sequence on a white background — perfect for:

✔ election pages
✔ results loading
✔ polling station lookup
✔ dashboard fetch states

If you’d like next (recommended):

                                    @forelse(($recentMessages ?? []) as $msg)
                                        <div class="msg-bx d-flex justify-content-between align-items-center">
                                            <div class="msg d-flex align-items-center w-100">
                                                <div class="image-box {{ $loop->first ? 'active' : '' }}">
                                                    <span class="bg-primary text-white d-inline-flex align-items-center justify-content-center"
                                                          style="width:40px;height:40px;border-radius:12px;">
                                                        {{ strtoupper(mb_substr($msg->name ?? 'U', 0, 1)) }}
                                                    </span>
                                                </div>
                                                <div class="ms-3 w-100">
                                                    <a href="{{ route('cms.inbox.show', $msg->id) }}">
                                                        <h5 class="mb-1">{{ $msg->name ?? 'Unknown Sender' }}</h5>
                                                    </a>
                                                    <div class="d-flex justify-content-between">
                                                        <p class="me-auto mb-0 text-black">
                                                            {{ \Illuminate\Support\Str::limit($msg->message ?? '', 60) }}
                                                        </p>
                                                        <small class="me-4">
                                                            {{ optional($msg->created_at)->diffForHumans() }}
