<!-- Modal -->
@push('modal')
<div class="modal fade" id="sendMessageModal">
    <div class="modal-dialog modal-dialog-centered" role="document">
        <div class="modal-content">
            <div class="modal-header">
                <h5 class="modal-title">Send Message</h5>
                <button type="button" class="btn-close" data-bs-dismiss="modal"></button>
            </div>
            <div class="modal-body">
                <form class="comment-form" method="POST" action="{{ route('cms.messages.send') }}">
                    @csrf
                    <div class="row">
                        <div class="col-lg-6">
                            <div class="mb-3">
                                <label class="text-black font-w600 form-label required">Name</label>
                                <input type="text" class="form-control" name="name" placeholder="Your name" required>
                            </div>
                        </div>
                        <div class="col-lg-6">
                            <div class="mb-3">
                                <label class="text-black font-w600 form-label">Email</label>
                                <input type="email" class="form-control" name="email" placeholder="Email">
                            </div>
                        </div>
                        <div class="col-lg-12">
                            <div class="mb-3">
                                <label class="text-black font-w600 form-label">Message</label>
                                <textarea rows="8" class="form-control" name="message" placeholder="Write message..." required></textarea>
                            </div>
                        </div>
                        <div class="col-lg-12">
                            <div class="mb-3 mb-0">
                                <button type="submit" class="submit btn btn-primary">Send</button>
                            </div>
                        </div>
                    </div>
                </form>
                <small class="text-muted d-block mt-3">
                    Tip: Use this for internal notes or to message site admins.
                </small>
            </div>
        </div>
    </div>
</div>
@endpush

@push('scripts')
<script>
    function cardsCenter() {
        jQuery('.card-slider').owlCarousel({
            loop: true,
            margin: 0,
            nav: true,
            slideSpeed: 3000,
            paginationSpeed: 3000,
            dots: true,
            navText: ['<i class="fas fa-arrow-left"></i>', '<i class="fas fa-arrow-right"></i>'],
            responsive: {
                0: { items: 1 },
                576: { items: 1 },
                800: { items: 1 },
                991: { items: 1 },
                1200: { items: 1 },
                1600: { items: 1 }
            }
        })
    }

    jQuery(window).on('load', function() {
        setTimeout(function() {
            cardsCenter();
        }, 1000);
    });
</script>
@endpush

<!-- Modal -->
@push('modal')
<div class="modal fade" id="sendMessageModal">
    <div class="modal-dialog modal-dialog-centered" role="document">
        <div class="modal-content">
            <div class="modal-header">
                <h5 class="modal-title">Send Message</h5>
                <button type="button" class="btn-close" data-bs-dismiss="modal"></button>
            </div>
            <div class="modal-body">
                <form class="comment-form" action="javascript:void(0)">
                    <div class="row">
                        <div class="col-lg-6">
                            <div class="mb-3">
                                <label class="text-black font-w600 form-label required">Name</label>
                                <input type="text" class="form-control" placeholder="Your name" value="Editor">
                            </div>
                        </div>
                        <div class="col-lg-6">
                            <div class="mb-3">
                                <label class="text-black font-w600 form-label">Email</label>
                                <input type="text" class="form-control" placeholder="Email" value="editor@example.com">
                            </div>
                        </div>
                        <div class="col-lg-12">
                            <div class="mb-3">
                                <label class="text-black font-w600 form-label">Message</label>
                                <textarea rows="8" class="form-control" placeholder="Write message...">Hi team, please review the homepage notice and update the banner.</textarea>
                            </div>
                        </div>
                        <div class="col-lg-12">
                            <div class="mb-3 mb-0">
                                <input type="submit" value="Send" class="submit btn btn-primary">
                            </div>
                        </div>
                    </div>
                </form>
                <small class="text-muted d-block mt-3">
                    This is a dummy form for UI only (no backend wired yet).
                </small>
            </div>
        </div>
    </div>
</div>
@endpush

@push('scripts')
<script>
    function cardsCenter() {
        jQuery('.card-slider').owlCarousel({
            loop: true,
            margin: 0,
            nav: true,
            slideSpeed: 3000,
            paginationSpeed: 3000,
            dots: true,
            navText: ['<i class="fas fa-arrow-left"></i>', '<i class="fas fa-arrow-right"></i>'],
            responsive: {
                0: { items: 1 },
                576: { items: 1 },
                800: { items: 1 },
                991: { items: 1 },
                1200: { items: 1 },
                1600: { items: 1 }
            }
        })
    }

    jQuery(window).on('load', function() {
        setTimeout(function() {
            cardsCenter();
        }, 1000);
    });
</script>
@endpush

You said:
Give these the ecz colors that i provided earlier: :root { --primary: #0c9154; --secondary: #f37221; --primary-light: #a590cf; --secondary-light: #ffdaee; --danger-light: #fd607c; --primary-hover: #6c4bae; --primary-dark: #402c67; --rgba-primary-1: rgba(136, 108, 192, 0.1); --rgba-primary-2: rgba(136, 108, 192, 0.2); --rgba-primary-3: rgba(136, 108, 192, 0.3); --rgba-primary-4: rgba(136, 108, 192, 0.4); --rgba-primary-5: rgba(136, 108, 192, 0.5); --rgba-primary-6: rgba(136, 108, 192, 0.6); --rgba-primary-7: rgba(136, 108, 192, 0.7); --rgba-primary-8: rgba(136, 108, 192, 0.8); --rgba-primary-9: rgba(136, 108, 192, 0.9); --rgba-secondary-1: rgba(255, 167, 215, 0.1); --font-family-base: Roboto, sans-serif; --font-family-title: Roboto, sans-serif; --title: #000; --gradient-one: linear-gradient(180deg, #FFA26D 0%, #FFCF6D 100%); --tryal-gradient:linear-gradient(212.43deg, #886CC0 19.43%, #AA6CC0 87.63%); --dz-sidebar-width: 16.5rem; --dz-header-height: 4.5rem; --dz-sidebar-pos: absolute; --dz-header-pos: relative; --dz-side-nav-padding: .85rem 1.5rem; --dz-side-nav-margin: 0 0; --dz-side-nav-size: 0.9375rem; --dz-side-ico-size: 1.2rem; --dz-side-ico-height: auto; --dz-side-ico-width: auto; --dz-side-sub_nav-padding: .4rem 1rem .4rem 4rem; --dz-side-sub_nav-size: 0.85rem; --dz-head-title-size: 1.25rem; --dz-side-menu-direction: column; --dz-side-menu-padding: 0 0; --dz-side-profile: block; --dz-side-info: block; --dz-side-copyright: block; }

ChatGPT said:
CSS
:root {
  /* ECZ Brand Colors */
  --primary: #0c9154;              /* Green */
  --secondary: #f37221;            /* Orange */
  --danger: #ed1c24;               /* Red */
  --accent: #db5012;               /* Deep Orange (optional accent you gave) */
  --title: #000;                   /* Black */
  --white: #fff;

  /* Light tints (soft UI backgrounds) */
  --primary-light: rgba(12, 145, 84, 0.12);
  --secondary-light: rgba(243, 114, 33, 0.14);
  --danger-light: rgba(237, 28, 36, 0.14);

  /* Hover / Dark tones (manually chosen to stay on-brand) */
  --primary-hover: #097a46;
  --primary-dark: #075f36;
  --secondary-hover: #db5012;      /* uses your provided #db5012 */
  --secondary-dark: #b9430f;
  --danger-hover: #c5161c;
  --danger-dark: #9f1015;

  /* Primary RGBA scale (replacing the purple-based ones) */
  --rgba-primary-1: rgba(12, 145, 84, 0.1);
  --rgba-primary-2: rgba(12, 145, 84, 0.2);
  --rgba-primary-3: rgba(12, 145, 84, 0.3);
  --rgba-primary-4: rgba(12, 145, 84, 0.4);
  --rgba-primary-5: rgba(12, 145, 84, 0.5);
  --rgba-primary-6: rgba(12, 145, 84, 0.6);
  --rgba-primary-7: rgba(12, 145, 84, 0.7);
  --rgba-primary-8: rgba(12, 145, 84, 0.8);
  --rgba-primary-9: rgba(12, 145, 84, 0.9);

  /* Secondary RGBA scale (replacing the pink-based one) */
  --rgba-secondary-1: rgba(243, 114, 33, 0.1);
  --rgba-secondary-2: rgba(243, 114, 33, 0.2);
  --rgba-secondary-3: rgba(243, 114, 33, 0.3);
  --rgba-secondary-4: rgba(243, 114, 33, 0.4);
  --rgba-secondary-5: rgba(243, 114, 33, 0.5);
  --rgba-secondary-6: rgba(243, 114, 33, 0.6);
  --rgba-secondary-7: rgba(243, 114, 33, 0.7);
  --rgba-secondary-8: rgba(243, 114, 33, 0.8);
  --rgba-secondary-9: rgba(243, 114, 33, 0.9);

  /* Optional danger RGBA scale (useful across the UI) */
  --rgba-danger-1: rgba(237, 28, 36, 0.1);
  --rgba-danger-2: rgba(237, 28, 36, 0.2);
  --rgba-danger-3: rgba(237, 28, 36, 0.3);
  --rgba-danger-4: rgba(237, 28, 36, 0.4);
  --rgba-danger-5: rgba(237, 28, 36, 0.5);
  --rgba-danger-6: rgba(237, 28, 36, 0.6);
  --rgba-danger-7: rgba(237, 28, 36, 0.7);
  --rgba-danger-8: rgba(237, 28, 36, 0.8);
  --rgba-danger-9: rgba(237, 28, 36, 0.9);

  /* Typography */
  --font-family-base: Roboto, sans-serif;
  --font-family-title: Roboto, sans-serif;

  /* Gradients updated to ECZ colors */
  --gradient-one: linear-gradient(180deg, #f37221 0%, #0c9154 100%);
  --tryal-gradient: linear-gradient(212.43deg, #0c9154 19.43%, #f37221 87.63%);

  /* Layout vars (unchanged) */
  --dz-sidebar-width: 16.5rem;
  --dz-header-height: 4.5rem;
  --dz-sidebar-pos: absolute;
  --dz-header-pos: relative;
  --dz-side-nav-padding: .85rem 1.5rem;
  --dz-side-nav-margin: 0 0;
  --dz-side-nav-size: 0.9375rem;
  --dz-side-ico-size: 1.2rem;
  --dz-side-ico-height: auto;
  --dz-side-ico-width: auto;
  --dz-side-sub_nav-padding: .4rem 1rem .4rem 4rem;
  --dz-side-sub_nav-size: 0.85rem;
  --dz-head-title-size: 1.25rem;
  --dz-side-menu-direction: column;
  --dz-side-menu-padding: 0 0;
  --dz-side-profile: block;
  --dz-side-info: block;
  --dz-side-copyright: block;
}

You said:
Use the green and orange here: .gradient_one { background-image: linear-gradient(to right, rgba(1, 121, 186, 0.85) 0%, rgba(103, 25, 255, 0.85) 100%); } .gradient-1 { background: #f0a907; background: -moz-linear-gradient(top, #f0a907 0%, #f53c79 100%); background: -webkit-linear-gradient(top, #f0a907 0%, #f53c79 100%); background: linear-gradient(to bottom, #f0a907 0%, #f53c79 100%) !important; filter: progid:DXImageTransform.Microsoft.gradient( startColorstr='#f0a907', endColorstr='#f53c79',GradientType=0 ); } .gradient-2 { background: #4dedf5; background: -moz-linear-gradient(top, #4dedf5 0%, #480ceb 100%); background: -webkit-linear-gradient(top, #4dedf5 0%, #480ceb 100%); background: linear-gradient(to bottom, #4dedf5 0%, #480ceb 100%) !important; filter: progid:DXImageTransform.Microsoft.gradient( startColorstr='#4dedf5', endColorstr='#480ceb',GradientType=0 ); } .gradient-3 { background: #51f5ae; background: -moz-linear-gradient(top, #51f5ae 0%, #3fbcda 100%);                                 <label class="form-check-label" for="basic_checkbox_1">Remember my preference</label>
                            </div>
                        </div>

                    </div>

PHP
// config/services.php
return [
    // ...
    'recaptcha' => [
        'site_key' => env('RECAPTCHA_SITE_KEY'),
        'secret_key' => env('RECAPTCHA_SECRET_KEY'),
    ],
];
env
# .env
RECAPTCHA_SITE_KEY=your_site_key_here
RECAPTCHA_SECRET_KEY=your_secret_key_here
PHP
// app/Http/Controllers/Auth/LoginController.php (or your login handler)
use Illuminate\Http\Request;
use Illuminate\Support\Facades\Http;

public function login(Request $request)
{
    $request->validate([
        'username' => ['required','string'],
        'password' => ['required','string'],
        'g-recaptcha-response' => ['required'],
    ], [
        'g-recaptcha-response.required' => 'Please complete the reCAPTCHA to continue.',
    ]);

    $verify = Http::asForm()->post('https://www.google.com/recaptcha/api/siteverify', [
        'secret'   => config('services.recaptcha.secret_key'),
        'response' => $request->input('g-recaptcha-response'),
        'remoteip' => $request->ip(),
    ])->json();

    if (empty($verify['success'])) {
        return back()
            ->withErrors(['g-recaptcha-response' => 'reCAPTCHA verification failed. Please try again.'])
            ->withInput($request->only('username'));
    }

    // continue with your normal login logic...
}

HTML
<span class="mb-30 d-inline-block">
   <svg ...>...</svg>
</span>
<h2 class="fs-70"><span data-purecounter-duration="1" data-purecounter-end="168"  class="purecounter">0</span>+</h2>
<p class="fw-500 fs-18 ls-m-2 tp-text-grey-2">Growth advice for your<br> consulting business</p>

With this:

HTML
<span class="mb-30 d-inline-block">
   <i class="ri-shield-check-line" style="font-size:60px; color:#000;"></i>
</span>

<h2 class="fs-70 mb-10">
   <span data-purecounter-duration="1" data-purecounter-end="14" class="purecounter">0</span>+
</h2>

<p class="fw-500 fs-18 ls-m-2 tp-text-grey-2 mb-0">
   Successful &amp; peaceful<br> national elections conducted
</p>

Change data-purecounter-end="14" to the exact number you want to display.

2) Add Remix Icon (only once)

Put this in your <head> (or your main layout head file):

HTML
<link href="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/remixicon@4.6.0/fonts/remixicon.css" rel="stylesheet">

3) (Optional) Use your ECZ colors on that badge

If you want that badge background to match your ECZ palette (green/orange), swap:

HTML
<div class="tp-bg-theme-secondary text-center pt-60 pb-35 tp-round-4 h-100">

to:

HTML
<div class="text-center pt-60 pb-35 tp-round-4 h-100" style="background:#f27121;">

Or green:

HTML
<div class="text-center pt-60 pb-35 tp-round-4 h-100" style="background:#0c9154;">

If you tell me the exact number of peaceful elections you want shown (or whether it should be “since 1991”), I’ll plug it in and adjust the wording to match that timeline perfectly.


You said:
i don't want the number to break in another line: <p class="mb-0 lh-24">+260 211 253155</p>

ChatGPT said:

Got it — that’s just a line-wrapping issue.

Add white-space: nowrap so the number never breaks.

✅ Option 1 (quick inline fix)

HTML
<p class="mb-0 lh-24" style="white-space: nowrap;">+260 211 253155</p>

✅ Option 2 (clean CSS approach — recommended)

Add this to your CSS:

CSS
.no-wrap {
    white-space: nowrap;
}

Then update your HTML:

HTML
<p class="mb-0 lh-24 no-wrap">+260 211 253155</p>

Optional (mobile-safe version)

If you also want smaller phones to scale nicely:

CSS
.no-wrap {
    white-space: nowrap;
    font-size: clamp(14px, 2vw, 16px);
}

This will:

✅ Keep the phone number on one line
✅ Prevent awkward wrapping
✅ Stay responsive

If you’d like, I can also add:

• clickable tel link
• copy-to-clipboard
• icon inline
• ECZ green/orange styling

Just tell me 👍


You said:
how can i add a comma for better display: <span data-purecounter-duration="1" data-purecounter-end="7073513" class="purecounter">0</span>

ChatGPT said:

Good question — since you’re using PureCounter, you don’t need JavaScript for this. PureCounter already supports thousand separators.

Just add the data-purecounter-separator attribute.

✅ Updated span (with comma formatting)

HTML
<span
    data-purecounter-duration="1"
    data-purecounter-end="7073513"
    data-purecounter-separator=","
    class="purecounter">0</span>

Result

Instead of:

Code
7073513

You’ll get:

Code
7,073,513

Animated nicely.

Optional (Zambia-style spacing instead of comma)

If you prefer spaces:

HTML
data-purecounter-separator=" "

→ 7 073 513

If you'd like next:

✅ add suffix like +
✅ add “Registered Voters” label
✅ animate decimals
✅ style digits in ECZ colors

Just tell me 👍

You can hand this directly to a copywriter — it tells them where, what, and how to write.

Website Content & Copywriting Brief

For the Electoral Commission of Zambia (ECZ) Official Website

📌 1. Navigation/Menu Structure

Purpose: Clear, user-friendly navigation that reflects key services and priorities of ECZ.

Current main areas (inferred from official site):

• Home
• About ECZ
• Elections
• Electoral Systems / Process
• Voter Information
• Voter Services
• News & Notices
• Contact

Suggested Modernized, User-Centered Navigation Labels

Current / TraditionalSuggested (Modern & Clear)
HomeHome
AboutAbout Us
ElectionsElection Information
Voter RegistrationVoters & Registration
Electoral SystemsHow Elections Work
NewsNews & Updates
DownloadsResources & Downloads
ContactContact Us
Work With ECZCareers & Tenders

Copywriter Notes:

  • Ensure navigation labels are short, action-oriented, and user-friendly.

  • Avoid internal jargon like “VERC” that may confuse first-time visitors.

📌 2. Hero / Home Page Copy

Goal: Introduce ECZ’s mission in a concise, authoritative tone — reassuring trust and clarity.

Suggested Headline

“Delivering Free, Fair & Peaceful Elections for Every Citizen”

Suggested Subhead

“The Electoral Commission of Zambia is responsible for managing credible, transparent and inclusive electoral processes.”

Copy Notes:

  • Emphasize independence, credibility, transparency.

  • Be public-centric — focus on voters and participation.

📌 3. About Us Page

Sections to write:

A. Our Mandate

  • Summarize ECZ’s legal mandate: implement process, conduct elections, manage disputes, voter education, delimitation and accreditation. Electoral Commission of Zambia - Home

B. Our History

  • Brief origin story: Established under the Constitution of Zambia; first elections after 1991 reforms; delivered multiple general elections. (Use historical context — e.g., from external reference: ECZ has managed General Elections since establishment). LinkedIn

C. Our Structure

  • Chairperson + Commissioners

  • CEO & Management Team

  • Role differentiation (policy vs operations)

D. Our Values

  • Independence, integrity, inclusivity, professionalism.

📌 4. Elections & Electoral Process

Subpages:

A. Types of Elections

  • Presidential

  • National Assembly

  • Local Government

B. Electoral Systems

  • First past the post and Majoritarian definitions. Electoral Commission of Zambia - Home

C. Voting Day Procedures

  • How to vote

  • What to bring

  • Location rules

Copy Tone:
Clear, non-technical, explanatory.

📌 5. Voter Information

Pages/Sections:

A. Who Can Vote

  • Eligibility (Zambian citizen, 18+, NRC requirements). Electoral Commission of Zambia - Home

B. Registration Status Check

  • Link to online verification. Voter Verification

C. Find Your Polling Station

  • Guidance on how to search and what to expect.

D. FAQs

  • Common questions about Election Day, results, timelines. Electoral Commission of Zambia - Home

📌 6. News & Updates

Required Copy Sections:

  • Headlines that are specific and descriptive (e.g., “ECZ Announces Inspection of Provisional Register”)

  • Summary paragraphs (2–3 lines) before “read more”

  • Dates and categories

Example Comms:

  • Deployment plans

  • Official statements

  • Notices of elections or by-elections

Copy Style:
Concise + factual + easy to scan.

📌 7. Resources & Downloads

Content to include:

  • 2023–2027 Strategic Plan

  • Election legal documents

  • Voter registers per polling station

  • Deployment plans and official publications Electoral Commission of Zambia - Home

Copy Tips:

  • Provide a short description for each download.

  • Include file size and type (PDF).

📌 8. Contact & Support

Elements to include:

  • Address & office hours

  • Phone and email contacts

  • A simple contact form

  • Clear instructions on how to report issues or ask questions Electoral Commission of Zambia - Home

📌 9. Careers & Work With ECZ

Suggested copy categories:

  • Current opportunities (e.g., “Technical Support Officers”). Electoral Commission of Zambia - Home

  • How to apply

  • Organizational benefits and impact

Copy tone:
Professional + encouraging public service.

📌 10. Footer Copy Elements

Include:

  • About snippet (1 sentence)

  • Quick links

  • Social media icons + links

  • Contact phone/email

  • Copyright + year

Example:

The Electoral Commission of Zambia administers credible elections, manages voter education, and safeguards democratic participation for all eligible citizens.

📌 General Copywriting Guidelines

✔ Use active, engaging language
✔ Avoid complex legal jargon — make it citizen-friendly
✔ Keep paragraphs short (1–3 sentences)
✔ Use bullets for lists
✔ Highlight key actions (e.g., “Find your polling station”)
✔ Include links/buttons with clear CTAs (Call to Action)

📌 Tone & Style

• Professional, inclusive, clear
• Citizen-centric (not institutional)
• Trustworthy and factual

Notes for the Copywriter

  • Refer to the official ECZ site for factual details. Electoral Commission of Zambia - Home

  • Ensure mobile readability and concise sentences

  • Navigation labels should reflect user intent (like “Election Info”, “Voter Services”, “Latest News”)

If you’d like, I can also export this to a Google Doc / Word template with placeholders and prompts per section. Just ask!

ChatGPT said:

Got it — let’s turn this into a proper professional copywriting brief that your writer can actually work from.

Electoral Commission of Zambia Website

Content & Copywriting Brief (Draft v1)

Introduction

This document serves as a comprehensive copywriting guide for the redesign of the Electoral Commission of Zambia (ECZ) website.

The goal is to modernize the language, structure, and presentation of content while maintaining the authority, neutrality, and credibility of the Commission.

The existing ECZ website (https://www.elections.org.zm/) should be used strictly as a factual reference source, ensuring legal accuracy, institutional tone, and completeness of information. However, the new website copy must:

  • Be more citizen-focused

  • Use plain, accessible language

  • Feel modern, transparent, and welcoming

  • Guide users clearly toward key actions (voting, finding polling stations, checking updates)

  • Reduce bureaucratic wording

  • Improve navigation clarity

  • Highlight ECZ’s record of credible, peaceful elections

  • Emphasize trust, independence, and service to the public

The copywriter’s role is not only to rewrite content — but to reframe ECZ as a service-oriented democratic institution, placing Zambian citizens at the center of every message.

Primary audiences:

  • Zambian voters

  • First-time voters (youth)

  • Media & observers

  • Political stakeholders

  • Civil society

  • International partners

Tone guidelines:

  • Professional but human

  • Authoritative but friendly

  • Clear, simple, and confident

  • Non-political and neutral

  • Forward-looking

SECTION 1 — Navigation & Website Structure

Objective

Create a navigation system that:

  • Feels modern and intuitive

  • Uses clear, everyday language

  • Reflects what citizens actually want to find

  • Reduces institutional jargon

  • Prioritizes voter needs

  • Works well on mobile

Navigation labels must be short, action-driven, and citizen-centered.

A. Proposed Main Navigation

Current style (traditional/institutional)

Typical government websites use labels like:

  • Home

  • About ECZ

  • Elections

  • Electoral Systems

  • Voter Registration

  • Downloads

  • Contact

These feel administrative and inward-facing.

B. Recommended Modern Navigation (User-Focused)

The copywriter should refine and suggest wording similar to:

Primary Menu

  1. Home

  2. About Us

  3. Elections
    (dropdown)

    • Upcoming Elections

    • Past Elections

    • Election Results

  4. Voters
    (dropdown)

    • Voter Information

    • How to Vote

    • Find Your Polling Station

    • Voter Education

  5. How Elections Work
    (dropdown)

    • Electoral Process

    • Voting Procedures

    • Result Verification

  6. News & Updates

  7. Resources
    (dropdown)

    • Downloads

    • Legal Documents

    • Publications

  8. Contact Us

C. Optional Utility Menu (Top Bar or Header)

Small secondary links:

  • Careers & Tenders

  • Media Centre

  • FAQs

D. CTA Buttons (Important)

Add one or two highlighted action buttons in the header:

Examples:

✅ “Find Your Polling Station”
✅ “Election Updates”
✅ “Voter Information”

These should stand out visually and be written as actions, not nouns.

Copywriter Tasks for Navigation

The copywriter should:

  1. Propose final wording for each menu item

  2. Suggest alternatives that feel more modern

  3. Remove bureaucratic terms where possible

  4. Ensure labels reflect user intent

  5. Keep everything under 3 words if possible

  6. Ensure consistency across desktop and mobile

Example:

Instead of:

Electoral Systems

Use:

How Elections Work

Instead of:

Downloads

Use:

Resources

Instead of:

Voter Registration

Use:

Voters & Registration

Navigation Tone Examples

Bad:

Electoral Delimitation Framework

Better:

Constituency Boundaries

Bad:

Verification of Electoral Register

Better:

Check Your Registration

That completes Section 1: Navigation & Site Structure.

and we’ll move to:

SECTION 2 — Homepage / Hero Area

Objective

The homepage hero is the emotional anchor of the entire site.

It must immediately communicate:

✅ Trust
✅ Authority
✅ National importance
✅ Citizen participation
✅ Peaceful democratic process

This is NOT a marketing hero.
It is a civic hero.

The copy should feel:

  • Inspiring but grounded

  • Professional yet human

  • Inclusive and forward-looking

  • Focused on Zambians first

Avoid corporate or consulting language.

Hero Headline (Primary Message)

The hero headline must clearly state ECZ’s purpose in one powerful sentence.

Reference themes from current ECZ site:

  • Credible elections

  • Transparency

  • Independence

  • Democratic participation

Suggested Direction (Examples Only)

The copywriter should refine or improve on ideas like:

Option A (Trust-focused)

Delivering Credible, Transparent Elections for Every Zambian

Option B (People-focused)

Safeguarding Zambia’s Democracy Through Free and Fair Elections

Option C (Action-focused)

Empowering Citizens. Protecting the Vote. Strengthening Democracy.

Option D (Service-focused)

Your Trusted Partner in Zambia’s Electoral Process

The copywriter should propose at least 3 headline variations.

Hero Subtext (Supporting Line)

This must explain ECZ in one short paragraph.

Example Direction:

The Electoral Commission of Zambia is an independent constitutional body responsible for conducting elections and referenda in a professional, impartial, and transparent manner — ensuring every citizen’s voice is heard.

Copywriter should:

  • Simplify wording

  • Remove legal jargon

  • Make it human

  • Keep under 35 words

Hero Call-To-Actions (CTAs)

Primary CTAs must be practical.

Avoid generic “Learn More”.

Recommended CTAs:

Primary:

  • Find Your Polling Station

  • Voter Information

Secondary:

  • Election Updates

  • How to Vote

The copywriter should:

  • Suggest final button labels

  • Ensure action-oriented wording

  • Keep labels short

Hero Image Guidance (For Context Only)

Images will show:

  • Happy Zambian voters

  • Polling stations

  • Election officials assisting citizens

  • Real community environments

Copy should match this tone:

👉 Human
👉 Optimistic
👉 Peaceful
👉 Authentic

Optional Hero Stats (If Used)

If stats appear in hero or just below:

Examples:

  • Successful elections conducted

  • Registered voters

  • Polling stations nationwide

Copywriter should craft micro-copy like:

“Over 7 million registered voters nationwide”

NOT:

“Registered voter statistics”

Hero Copywriting Tasks

The copywriter must deliver:

  1. 3–5 hero headline options

  2. Supporting paragraph

  3. Primary CTA wording

  4. Secondary CTA wording

  5. Optional stat captions

That completes Section 2 — Homepage / Hero Area.

SECTION 3 — About ECZ / Who We Are

Objective

This section explains:

✅ Who ECZ is
✅ Why ECZ exists
✅ What ECZ stands for
✅ How ECZ serves Zambians

It must feel:

  • Institutional, but approachable

  • Authoritative, not bureaucratic

  • National, not corporate

Avoid “consulting”, “solutions”, or “clients”.

Use language that reflects:

  • Constitutional mandate

  • Public service

  • National responsibility

  • Democratic values

Section Heading

Current direction:

Delivering credible, transparent and inclusive electoral processes

This is strong — but can be modernized.

Copywriter should propose alternatives such as:

Examples:

Who We Are

or

About the Electoral Commission of Zambia

or

Serving Zambia’s Democracy

Core About Paragraph

Reference from elections.org.zm:

ECZ is an independent constitutional body responsible for conducting elections.

This must be rewritten in modern, clear language.

Sample Direction:

The Electoral Commission of Zambia (ECZ) is an independent constitutional body mandated to manage and conduct elections and referenda. We work to ensure every electoral process is free, fair, transparent, and accessible — giving every Zambian the confidence to participate in shaping the nation’s future.

Copywriter should:

  • Reduce legal language

  • Improve flow

  • Keep under ~80 words

Key Pillars (Suggested 3–4 Highlights)

Displayed visually on site.

Copywriter to refine wording for:

Example Pillars:

1. Independence

We operate free from political influence.

2. Transparency

Every process is open, accountable, and verifiable.

3. Integrity

We uphold the highest ethical standards.

4. Inclusion

Every eligible citizen deserves a voice.

Each pillar should have:

  • Short title

  • One-line description

“By the Numbers” Section

Currently replacing consulting stats.

Should reflect:

  • Successful elections conducted

  • Years of service

  • Registered voters

  • Polling stations

Example structure:

Example:

7+ National Elections Conducted

7 Million+ Registered Voters

Thousands of Polling Stations Nationwide

Copywriter to:

  • Propose final numbers (ECZ to confirm)

  • Write short captions

About Section CTA

Examples:

  • Learn More About ECZ

  • Our Mandate

  • Explore Our Work

Copywriter to suggest best phrasing.

About Section Tasks for Copywriter

They must provide:

  1. Section heading options

  2. Core about paragraph

  3. 3–4 value pillars + descriptions

  4. Stat captions

  5. CTA wording

That completes Section 3 — About ECZ.

SECTION 4 — Our Mandate & Responsibilities

Objective

This section explains what ECZ is legally and operationally responsible for.

It must:

  • Clearly reflect ECZ’s constitutional mandate

  • Demonstrate authority and competence

  • Show breadth of responsibilities

  • Reassure citizens about accountability

Tone must be:

  • Clear

  • Structured

  • Neutral

  • Professional

Avoid heavy legal jargon.
Avoid copying constitutional language verbatim.
Translate legal mandate into citizen-friendly explanations.

Section Heading Options

The copywriter should propose modern, clear headings such as:

  • Our Mandate

  • What We Do

  • Our Constitutional Role

  • Our Responsibilities

Avoid overly technical titles.

Opening Paragraph (Context)

This paragraph must summarize ECZ’s legal foundation.

Direction example:

The Electoral Commission of Zambia is established under the Constitution of Zambia and mandated to organize, conduct, and supervise elections and referenda. Our responsibility is to ensure that every electoral process is credible, transparent, and conducted in accordance with the law.

Copywriter must:

  • Keep it under 100 words

  • Make it clear and accessible

  • Maintain institutional authority

Core Responsibilities (Structured List)

This section should visually present responsibilities in clean blocks or cards.

Copywriter should expand each item into 1–2 sentence explanations.

1. Conducting Elections

Explanation should cover:

  • Presidential elections

  • National Assembly elections

  • Local Government elections

  • Referenda

Example direction:

Organizing and managing Presidential, Parliamentary, Local Government elections and national referenda in accordance with the Constitution.

2. Voter Registration

Explanation should cover:

  • Registration of eligible citizens

  • Maintenance of the voter register

  • Inspection and verification processes

3. Delimitation of Boundaries

Explanation should cover:

  • Review of constituencies and wards

  • Ensuring fair representation

Make this understandable to non-political audiences.

4. Election Monitoring & Oversight

Explanation should cover:

  • Accreditation of observers

  • Oversight of polling stations

  • Compliance with electoral laws

5. Announcement of Results

Explanation should emphasize:

  • Official declaration

  • Transparency

  • Legal compliance

6. Voter Education

This is critical and should be highlighted strongly.

Explanation should cover:

  • Public awareness

  • Civic education

  • Youth engagement

Visual Enhancement Suggestion

Each responsibility should have:

  • Simple icon

  • Short heading

  • 1–2 line explanation

Avoid long paragraphs.

Accountability & Transparency Statement

This should close the section.

Example direction:

We are accountable to the people of Zambia and committed to conducting elections that reflect the will of the electorate with integrity and professionalism.

Copywriter should refine this.

Tasks for the Copywriter

They must deliver:

  1. Final section heading

  2. Opening mandate paragraph

  3. Refined responsibility titles

  4. 1–2 sentence explanation per responsibility

  5. Closing accountability statement

That completes Section 4 — Our Mandate & Responsibilities.

Next up:

Section 5 — Elections (Types, Cycles & Structure)

SECTION 5 — Elections (Types, Cycles & Structure)

Objective

This section explains how elections work in Zambia and what types of elections ECZ manages.

It should help citizens understand:

  • What elections exist

  • When they occur

  • How they are structured

  • Who participates

Tone:

  • Informative

  • Simple

  • Civic-focused

  • Trust-building

This is a high-traffic section during election periods, so clarity is critical.

Section Heading Options

Copywriter should propose modern, user-friendly headings such as:

  • Elections in Zambia

  • Understanding Our Electoral Process

  • Types of Elections

  • How Elections Work

Avoid formal/legal titles.

Intro Paragraph (Plain Language)

This opening explains Zambia’s election cycle.

Direction example:

Zambia conducts regular elections to choose national and local leaders. These elections are held every five years and allow citizens to participate directly in shaping their government.

Keep this short (2–3 sentences).

Election Types (Primary Content Block)

Present as cards or sections.

Each type must have:

  • Title

  • Short description

  • Simple icon

Presidential Elections

Purpose:

  • Elect the President

Copy direction:

Presidential elections allow citizens to elect the Head of State and Government every five years.

Parliamentary Elections

Purpose:

  • Elect Members of Parliament

Copy direction:

Parliamentary elections choose representatives for Zambia’s National Assembly from constituencies across the country.

Local Government Elections

Purpose:

  • Elect Mayors, Council Chairpersons, Councillors

Copy direction:

Local Government elections enable communities to elect leaders who manage local development and services.

Referenda (When Applicable)

Purpose:

  • Vote on constitutional or national matters

Copy direction:

Referenda allow citizens to vote directly on important national issues when required by law.

Election Cycle Overview

This section should visually show:

  1. Voter registration

  2. Nomination of candidates

  3. Campaign period

  4. Polling day

  5. Results announcement

Copywriter must provide brief labels for each stage.

Who Can Vote

Short eligibility explanation:

  • Zambian citizen

  • 18 years and above

  • Registered voter

Example direction:

To vote, one must be a Zambian citizen aged 18 or older and registered in the voter register.

Participation Encouragement Statement

Close with civic motivation.

Example direction:

Voting is both a right and a responsibility. Every eligible citizen is encouraged to participate peacefully and responsibly.

Tasks for the Copywriter

They must provide:

  1. Final section heading

  2. Intro paragraph

  3. Election type descriptions

  4. Election cycle labels

  5. Voter eligibility paragraph

  6. Participation closing statement

That completes Section 5 — Elections (Types, Cycles & Structure).

(polling stations, voter status, results, notices)

SECTION 6 — Voter Services & Public Tools

Objective

This section focuses on practical services citizens actively look for, especially close to elections.

It must answer:

  • Where do I vote?

  • Am I registered?

  • What’s happening today?

  • Where are results posted?

This is one of the most important usability sections on the site.

Tone:

  • Direct

  • Action-oriented

  • Simple language

  • Citizen-first

Avoid bureaucracy.

Section Heading Options

Copywriter to modernize:

  • Voter Services

  • Citizen Services

  • Your Voting Tools

  • Election Services

Avoid: “Public Services” or “Information Portal.”

Core Service Cards / Blocks

These should appear as clickable cards on the site.

Each requires:

  • Short title

  • 1–2 line explanation

  • Action button label

🗳️ Find Your Polling Station

Purpose:
Help voters locate where they will vote.

Copy direction:

Title:
Find Your Polling Station

Description:

Search for your assigned polling station by entering your NRC or voter details.

Button ideas:

  • Find My Station

  • Locate Polling Station

✅ Check Voter Status

Purpose:
Confirm registration and voting eligibility.

Copy direction:

Title:
Check Voter Status

Description:

Verify your voter registration status online quickly and securely.

Buttons:

  • Check Status

  • Verify Registration

📄 Election Notices & Updates

Purpose:
Publish official announcements.

Copy direction:

Title:
Election Notices

Description:

Stay informed with official election announcements, timelines, and public notices.

Buttons:

  • View Notices

  • Latest Updates

📊 Results Portal

Purpose:
Live and archived results.

Copy direction:

Title:
Election Results

Description:

Access verified election results as they are announced by the Commission.

Buttons:

  • View Results

  • Results Portal

📰 Media & Publications

Purpose:
Reports, documents, press releases.

Copy direction:

Title:
Media & Publications

Description:

Download reports, press statements, voter education materials, and official publications.

Buttons:

  • View Publications

  • Media Centre

Accessibility Emphasis

Include accessibility copy:

These services are available online and at ECZ offices nationwide.

Optional line:

Support is available for persons with disabilities and elderly voters.

Emergency / Election Day Banner

Optional highlighted banner during elections:

Having trouble voting? Call our toll-free voter support line.

Copywriter should suggest wording.

Tasks for Copywriter

They must supply:

  1. Section heading

  2. Card titles

  3. Descriptions

  4. Button text

  5. Accessibility statement

  6. Election-day alert banner text

That completes Section 6 — Voter Services & Public Tools.

Next:

Section 7 — Transparency, Integrity & Security

Say:

👉 Next section

when ready.


You said:
next section

ChatGPT said:

Great — continuing.

SECTION 7 — Transparency, Integrity & Security

Purpose

This section builds public trust.

It answers:

  • How are elections protected?

  • Who oversees the process?

  • How does ECZ ensure fairness?

  • What safeguards exist against fraud?

Tone:

  • Authoritative

  • Reassuring

  • Professional

  • Clear

Avoid heavy legal language.

This section should feel institutional but human.

Suggested Section Heading

Copywriter to refine:

  • Transparency & Accountability

  • Election Integrity

  • Trust in the Process

  • Safeguarding Democracy

Intro Paragraph

Short trust statement.

Example direction:

The Electoral Commission of Zambia is committed to conducting elections that are credible, transparent, and inclusive. Every stage of the electoral process is governed by law and supported by independent oversight mechanisms to protect the will of the people.

Copywriter should modernize this.

Sub-Sections

🔐 Electoral Safeguards

Explain systems in place:

Topics:

  • Secure voter registers

  • Ballot handling procedures

  • Polling station controls

  • Result verification

  • Observer accreditation

Copy direction:

From voter registration to final results, every step follows strict procedures designed to safeguard accuracy and fairness.

👁️ Independent Monitoring

Explain observer roles:

  • Local observers

  • International observers

  • Political party agents

Copy direction:

Elections are monitored by accredited observers and party agents to ensure transparency and accountability at all polling stages.

⚖️ Legal Framework

Explain law compliance:

Topics:

  • Electoral Act

  • Regulations

  • Dispute resolution

Copy direction:

All electoral activities are conducted in accordance with Zambia’s electoral laws and regulations, with established channels for complaints and appeals.

📊 Results Verification Process

Explain multi-layer verification:

Topics:

  • Polling station results

  • Constituency aggregation

  • National verification

Copy direction:

Results are verified through a structured multi-level process before official announcement.

📢 Reporting Irregularities

Encourage citizen participation:

Copy direction:

Citizens are encouraged to report electoral concerns through official ECZ channels for immediate investigation.

Buttons:

  • Report an Issue

  • Contact Electoral Support

Trust Statistics Block (Optional Visual)

Example:

  • X successful elections conducted

  • Nationwide polling coverage

  • Thousands of trained officials

Copywriter to supply figures.

Tasks for Copywriter

Provide:

  1. Main section headline

  2. Intro paragraph

  3. Safeguards explanation

  4. Observer description

  5. Legal framework copy

  6. Results verification explanation

  7. Citizen reporting text

  8. Trust statistic captions

That completes Section 7 — Transparency, Integrity & Security.

Next:

Section 8 — News, Updates & Media Centre

Say:

👉 Next section

when ready.


You said:
Next

ChatGPT said:

Got it — continuing.

SECTION 8 — News, Updates & Media Centre

Purpose

This section keeps the public informed in real time.

It should feel:

  • Current

  • Reliable

  • Accessible

  • Media-friendly

This is where journalists, stakeholders, and citizens come for:

  • Official announcements

  • Press statements

  • Election updates

  • Public notices

Tone:

Clear. Neutral. Professional. Not promotional.

Suggested Section Headings (Copywriter to refine)

Primary heading options:

  • News & Updates

  • Media Centre

  • Latest Announcements

  • ECZ Newsroom

Subheading ideas:

  • Stay informed with official election updates

  • Verified information from the Electoral Commission

Intro Paragraph

Short and factual.

Direction:

Access official announcements, press releases, and election updates from the Electoral Commission of Zambia. This section provides timely information to keep citizens, stakeholders, and media partners informed.

Content Types Needed

Copywriter must write micro-copy for each content type.

📰 Latest News

Purpose: General public updates.

Examples:

  • Election timelines

  • Public notices

  • Registration announcements (future)

  • Operational updates

Card fields:

  • Headline

  • Short summary (2–3 lines)

  • Publish date

  • “Read more” CTA

CTA wording ideas:

  • Read update

  • View details

  • Learn more

📢 Press Releases

For media houses.

Copy direction:

Official statements issued by the Commission regarding electoral activities and national processes.

Each press item:

  • Title

  • Date

  • Download PDF

  • Read online

CTA options:

  • Download statement

  • View press release

🎥 Media Gallery

Images & videos.

Sections:

  • Photo Gallery

  • Video Highlights

Copy direction:

Browse official photos and videos from voter education activities, polling day operations, and national announcements.

📄 Public Notices

For legal or procedural announcements.

Examples:

  • Voter notices

  • Party notices

  • Regulatory updates

Copy direction:

Important public notices issued in accordance with electoral regulations.

Filtering & Search (UX Copy)

Buttons:

  • All

  • Press

  • Notices

  • Media

Search placeholder:

Search news or announcements…

Featured Highlight Block (Top Story)

One main featured article.

Copy direction:

Featured update — the latest major announcement from the Commission.

CTA:

  • View full story

Suggested Microcopy

Loading:

  • Loading updates…

Empty state:

  • No announcements available at this time.

Pagination:

  • Load more

  • Older posts

Tasks for Copywriter

Deliver:

  1. Main section heading

  2. Intro paragraph

  3. News card summaries template

  4. Press release description text

  5. Gallery intro copy

  6. Public notices description

  7. CTA wording

  8. Empty-state messaging

  9. Search placeholder text

That completes Section 8 — News, Updates & Media Centre.

Next:

Section 9 — Contact, Support & Public Enquiries

Say:

👉 Next section

when ready.


You said:
9

ChatGPT said:

Perfect — continuing.

SECTION 9 — Contact, Support & Public Enquiries

Purpose

This section is the primary communication bridge between ECZ and the public.

It must feel:

  • Welcoming

  • Clear

  • Trustworthy

  • Easy to use

Citizens should instantly know:

  • How to reach ECZ

  • What channel to use

  • What response to expect

This is NOT just a contact page — it is a public service access point.

Core Goals

  1. Reduce confusion

  2. Guide users to the right department

  3. Encourage legitimate enquiries

  4. Minimise repeat questions

  5. Support transparency

Main Heading Ideas

Copywriter to refine:

  • Contact the Electoral Commission

  • Get in Touch

  • Public Enquiries & Support

  • We’re Here to Help

Subheading ideas:

  • Reach us for election-related enquiries and official assistance

  • Connect with the Commission through verified channels

Intro Paragraph

Professional and reassuring.

Direction:

For enquiries, feedback, or official communication with the Electoral Commission of Zambia, please use the channels below. Our team is available to support citizens, stakeholders, and media partners.

Contact Blocks Needed

Each block requires short supporting copy.

📍 Physical Address

Copy direction:

Visit our headquarters or provincial offices for official services and documentation.

Fields:

  • Head office address

  • Provincial offices link

CTA:

  • View office locations

📞 Phone Support

Copy direction:

Speak directly with our support team during working hours.

Display format:

  • Main hotline

  • Alternative numbers

Microcopy:

  • Monday – Friday | 08:00 – 17:00

📧 Email Communication

Copy direction:

For formal enquiries and documentation, contact us via email.

Examples:

  • General enquiries

  • Media

  • Complaints

CTA:

  • Send email

🌐 Online Contact Form

Primary digital channel.

Intro:

Submit your enquiry using the form below and our team will respond as soon as possible.

Form labels:

  • Full Name

  • Email Address

  • Phone Number

  • Subject

  • Message

Submit button:

  • Send Message

  • Submit Enquiry

Success message:

  • Thank you — your message has been received.

Error message:

  • Please complete all required fields.

Categorised Enquiries (Important UX Addition)

Highly recommended.

Dropdown options:

  • General enquiry

  • Polling station information

  • Political party matters

  • Media enquiry

  • Complaints & reports

  • Other

Helper text:

Select the category that best matches your enquiry.

Complaints & Reporting Section

Separate block for integrity.

Copy direction:

The Commission provides confidential channels for reporting electoral concerns or misconduct.

CTA options:

  • Report an issue

  • Submit complaint

Supporting line:

All reports are treated with confidentiality.

Map Integration

Above or below contact form.

Copy direction:

Locate ECZ offices nationwide using the interactive map.

Social Channels (Optional)

Only verified accounts.

Copy direction:

Follow official ECZ platforms for real-time updates.

Expected Response Time (Trust Builder)

Small but powerful.

Example:

We aim to respond to all enquiries within 48 working hours.

Footer Contact Microcopy

Used site-wide.

Examples:

  • Need help? Contact ECZ

  • Official enquiries only via this website

Tasks for Copywriter

Deliver:

  1. Main heading + subheading

  2. Intro paragraph

  3. Address block text

  4. Phone support copy

  5. Email support copy

  6. Contact form labels

  7. Form success + error messages

  8. Enquiry category options

  9. Complaints section copy

  10. Map caption

  11. Response-time disclaimer

That completes Section 9 — Contact, Support & Public Enquiries.

Next:

Section 10 — Footer Content & Trust Elements

Say:

👉 Next

when ready.


You said:
Section 10 — Footer Content & Trust Elements

ChatGPT said:

Got you — here’s the final major section.

SECTION 10 — Footer Content & Trust Elements

Purpose

The footer is not “just the bottom of the page.”

For ECZ, it is:

  • A credibility anchor

  • A navigation safety net

  • A legal reference point

  • A trust reinforcement layer

Many users scroll directly to the footer looking for:

  • Contact info

  • Legal links

  • Social platforms

  • Official confirmation

So this section must feel:

✔ Authoritative
✔ Clean
✔ Government-grade
✔ Transparent

Layout Recommendation

Four-column structure:

Column 1 — About ECZ

Short institutional summary.

Copy direction:

The Electoral Commission of Zambia is an independent constitutional body mandated to conduct and supervise elections and referenda in Zambia, ensuring credible, transparent, and inclusive democratic processes.

Optional tagline:

Upholding democracy through integrity and professionalism.

Column 2 — Quick Links

These should mirror main navigation but be simplified.

Suggested labels (copywriter may refine):

  • Home

  • About ECZ

  • Elections

  • Voter Information

  • Political Parties

  • Media Centre

  • Public Notices

  • Contact Us

Modern alternatives to test:

  • Discover ECZ

  • Election Services

  • Voter Hub

  • Transparency Centre

Column 3 — Resources

Practical information.

Examples:

  • Election Results

  • Polling Station Finder

  • Forms & Downloads

  • Legal Framework

  • FAQs

Optional:

  • Accessibility Statement

  • Privacy Policy

  • Terms of Use

Column 4 — Contact & Social

Structured contact:

Head Office
Short address line

Phone
Primary hotline

Email
General enquiries

Then social icons:

  • Facebook

  • X (Twitter)

  • YouTube

Only verified links.

Trust Elements (Very Important)

These should appear below main footer.

Legal Disclaimer

Copy direction:

This is the official website of the Electoral Commission of Zambia.

Optional extended version:

The Electoral Commission of Zambia does not accept responsibility for content on external websites linked from this platform.

Copyright Line

Example:

© 2026 Electoral Commission of Zambia. All rights reserved.

Transparency Badge Area (Optional)

Small icons or text:

  • Independent Constitutional Body

  • Verified Government Platform

  • Secure Website

Accessibility Statement (Recommended)

Short link:

Accessibility

Page copy direction:

ECZ is committed to providing accessible digital services to all citizens, including persons with disabilities.

Developer Credits (Optional)

Small subtle line:

Website developed by Rideve Media

(Only if approved.)

Micro Footer (Mobile)

Must still show:

  • ECZ name

  • Contact link

  • Privacy policy

Tasks for Copywriter

Deliver:

  1. About ECZ footer summary

  2. Quick Links naming options

  3. Resource links naming

  4. Legal disclaimer copy

  5. Copyright line

  6. Accessibility statement

  7. Trust badge labels

  8. Micro footer text

Navigation Modernisation (Global Recommendation)

Replace traditional labels with clearer citizen language:

Instead of:

  • Media
    Use:

  • News & Updates

Instead of:

  • About

Use:

  • Who We Are

Instead of:

  • Contact

Use:

  • Get Help

Instead of:

  • Political Parties

Use:

  • Registered Parties

Instead of:

  • Results

Use:

  • Election Results

Final Note for Copywriter

Tone must remain:

  • Neutral

  • Institutional

  • Citizen-first

  • Politically independent

Avoid:

❌ Marketing language
❌ Sales tone
❌ Emotional persuasion

This is a constitutional body.

That completes Section 10 — Footer Content & Trust Elements
and finishes the full copywriting framework.

If you’d like next, I can also provide:

✅ Full navigation rewrite proposal
✅ Homepage copy sample
✅ FAQ rewrite
✅ Hero slogans
✅ Microcopy library (buttons, labels, alerts)
✅ Accessibility wording
✅ Error-state messaging

Just tell me 👍


You said:
Create an image that shows an illustration of the body structure of a website that i can attatch with this document so that the people that are not conversant with the technical names of the sections can use as reference to understand

ChatGPT said:

You said:
create an eamil that sends the document and the image to the copywriter

ChatGPT said:

Sure — here’s a professional, clear email you can use to send the document and the website structure illustration to your copywriter:

Email
Subject
ECZ Website Copywriting Brief + Page Structure Reference

Hi [Copywriter’s Name],

I hope you’re well.

Please find attached the ECZ Website Copywriting Brief document, along with a visual illustration showing the overall body structure of the website. The illustration is meant to help you easily reference each section, especially where technical names may be unfamiliar.

The document outlines all required sections (from navigation through to footer), highlights areas that need refined copy, and provides guidance based on the current ECZ website — with the goal of creating more modern, citizen-friendly, and engaging content.

Key expectations:

• Refresh all section copy to feel clear, trustworthy, and contemporary
• Suggest improved navigation labels and button wording
• Maintain institutional credibility while using more human-centered language
• Ensure content is accessible to the general public
• Align tone with transparency, democracy, and civic participation

Please feel free to recommend alternative headings, CTAs, and section names where appropriate.

Let me know if anything needs clarification. Looking forward to reviewing your first draft.

Kind regards,
Ernest Lubinda

ChatGPT said:
HTML
<div class="tp-feature-area tp-feature-4-wrap pt-100 fix" id="vision-mission-values">
  <div class="container-fluid container-1690">
    <div class="tp-border-bottom">
      <div class="row gx-90">

        <!-- VISION -->
        <div class="col-lg-4 col-md-6 col-sm-6 tp-counter-3-border">
          <div class="tp-feature-3-wrap tpshake-wrap md-space d-flex">
            <span class="mr-30">
              <!-- Remix Icon -->
              <i class="ri-eye-line" style="font-size: 40px; color: #fff;"></i>
            </span>
            <div>
              <h3 class="fs-24 fw-500 mb-15">Our Vision</h3>
              <p>
                A credible, transparent and inclusive electoral process that reflects the will of the people.
              </p>
            </div>
          </div>
        </div>

        <!-- MISSION -->
        <div class="col-lg-4 col-md-6 col-sm-6 tp-counter-3-border">
          <div class="tp-feature-3-wrap tpshake-wrap d-flex md-space pl-15">
            <span class="mr-30">
              <!-- Remix Icon -->
              <i class="ri-flag-2-line" style="font-size: 40px; color: #fff;"></i>
            </span>
            <div>
              <h3 class="fs-24 fw-500 mb-15">Our Mission</h3>
              <p>
                To deliver credible elections through efficient voter services, impartial administration,
                and stakeholder engagement in line with the Constitution and electoral laws.
              </p>
            </div>
          </div>
        </div>

        <!-- CORE VALUES -->
        <div class="col-lg-4 col-md-6 col-sm-6">
          <div class="tp-feature-3-wrap tpshake-wrap d-flex pl-15">
            <span class="mr-30">
              <!-- Remix Icon -->
              <i class="ri-shield-check-line" style="font-size: 40px; color: #fff;"></i>
            </span>
            <div>
              <h3 class="fs-24 fw-500 mb-15">Our Core Values</h3>
              <p class="mb-0">
                Integrity, impartiality, professionalism, transparency, accountability, and public trust.
              </p>
            </div>
          </div>
        </div>

      </div>
    </div>
  </div>
</div>

Notes (quick):

  • Make sure Remix Icons is loaded in your <head>:

HTML
<link href="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/remixicon@4.2.0/fonts/remixicon.css" rel="stylesheet">

If you want, I can also style each card background to match ECZ (green/orange/red accents) while keeping the icons white.


You said:
You haven't put the the core values: OUR CORE VALUES We conduct our business with honesty and openness and are accountable to those we serve. We conduct our business in an honest, truthful and ethical manner. We are committed to treating our stakeholders in a fair, objective and unbiased manner. We create an enabling environment that embraces diversity and offers respect for everyone. We will endeavour to ensure that the electoral process and outcomes are trusted and acceptable to the general public. We rely on the collective skills and competencies in the discharge of our mandate. We seek new ways of doing business so as to achieve operational efficiency and effectiveness. We strive to consistently achieve high standards of electoral services through our competence, behaviour, attitude, and communication.

ChatGPT said:

You’re right — I only summarized them. Here’s the updated block with your full Core Values included (as a clean list inside the 3rd card).

HTML
<div class="tp-feature-area tp-feature-4-wrap pt-100 fix" id="vision-mission-values">
  <div class="container-fluid container-1690">
    <div class="tp-border-bottom">
      <div class="row gx-90">

        <!-- VISION -->
        <div class="col-lg-4 col-md-6 col-sm-6 tp-counter-3-border">
          <div class="tp-feature-3-wrap tpshake-wrap md-space d-flex">
            <span class="mr-30">
              <i class="ri-eye-line" style="font-size: 40px; color: #fff;"></i>
            </span>
            <div>
              <h3 class="fs-24 fw-500 mb-15">Our Vision</h3>
              <p>
                A credible, transparent and inclusive electoral process that reflects the will of the people.
              </p>
            </div>
          </div>
        </div>

        <!-- MISSION -->
        <div class="col-lg-4 col-md-6 col-sm-6 tp-counter-3-border">
          <div class="tp-feature-3-wrap tpshake-wrap d-flex md-space pl-15">
            <span class="mr-30">
              <i class="ri-flag-2-line" style="font-size: 40px; color: #fff;"></i>
            </span>
            <div>
              <h3 class="fs-24 fw-500 mb-15">Our Mission</h3>
              <p>
                To deliver credible elections through efficient voter services, impartial administration,
                and stakeholder engagement in line with the Constitution and electoral laws.
              </p>
            </div>
          </div>
        </div>

        <!-- CORE VALUES -->
        <div class="col-lg-4 col-md-12 col-sm-12">
          <div class="tp-feature-3-wrap tpshake-wrap d-flex pl-15">
            <span class="mr-30">
              <i class="ri-shield-check-line" style="font-size: 40px; color: #fff;"></i>
            </span>
            <div>
              <h3 class="fs-24 fw-500 mb-15">Our Core Values</h3>

              <ul class="mb-0" style="padding-left: 18px; margin: 0;">
                <li class="mb-10">
                  We conduct our business with honesty and openness and are accountable to those we serve.
                </li>
                <li class="mb-10">
                  We conduct our business in an honest, truthful and ethical manner.
                </li>
                <li class="mb-10">
                  We are committed to treating our stakeholders in a fair, objective and unbiased manner.
                </li>
                <li class="mb-10">
                  We create an enabling environment that embraces diversity and offers respect for everyone.
                </li>
                <li class="mb-10">
                  We will endeavour to ensure that the electoral process and outcomes are trusted and acceptable to the general public.
                </li>
                <li class="mb-10">
                  We rely on the collective skills and competencies in the discharge of our mandate.
                </li>
                <li class="mb-10">
                  We seek new ways of doing business so as to achieve operational efficiency and effectiveness.
                </li>
                <li>
                  We strive to consistently achieve high standards of electoral services through our competence, behaviour, attitude, and communication.
                </li>
              </ul>

            </div>
          </div>
        </div>

      </div>
    </div>
  </div>
</div>

If you want this to look less “bulky” on desktop, I can:

  • keep only 3–4 values visible

  • add a “Read more” collapse (Bootstrap collapse) for the rest (still SEO-friendly)

HTML
<!-- ABOUT US (ECZ Version) -->
<div class="tp-about-area pt-140 pb-110" id="about">
  <div class="container-fluid container-1690 p-relative">

    <!-- decorative icon -->
    <span class="tp-about-4-icon p-absolute mb-30 d-none d-lg-block">
      <svg width="180" height="180" viewBox="0 0 180 180" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">
        <path d="M100 100H180V180H100V100Z" fill="white" />
        <path d="M40 40H96C98.2091 40 100 41.7909 100 44V100H44C41.7909 100 40 98.2091 40 96V40Z" fill="white" />
        <path d="M0 0H36C38.2091 0 40 1.79086 40 4V40H4C1.79086 40 0 38.2091 0 36V0Z" fill="white" />
      </svg>
    </span>

    <div class="row align-items-center">

      <!-- STATS -->
      <div class="col-lg-3 col-md-5 mb-30">
        <div class="tp-about-4-count mr-110">

          <!-- Stat 1 -->
          <div class="tp-about-4-count-item mb-50 pb-25 tp-border-bottom d-block">
            <span class="d-inline-block mb-15 wow zoomIn" data-wow-duration="1.5s" data-wow-delay="0.2s">
              <i class="ri-shield-check-line" style="font-size:40px;color:#1F3130;"></i>
            </span>

            <!-- IMPORTANT: set the number you want here -->
            <h2 class="fs-80 ls-m-3 mb-0">
              <span data-purecounter-duration="1" data-purecounter-end="60" class="purecounter">0</span>+
            </h2>
            <p class="fw-500">
              Years of administering elections and supporting democratic governance.
            </p>
          </div>

          <!-- Stat 2 -->
          <div class="tp-about-4-count-item d-block">
            <span class="d-inline-block mb-15">
              <i class="ri-map-pin-2-line" style="font-size:40px;color:#1F3130;"></i>
            </span>

            <!-- IMPORTANT: set the number you want here -->
            <h2 class="fs-80 ls-m-3 mb-0">
              <span data-purecounter-duration="1" data-purecounter-end="116" class="purecounter">0</span>+
            </h2>
            <p class="fw-500">
              Districts served nationwide through voter services and electoral operations.
            </p>
          </div>

        </div>
      </div>

      <!-- IMAGE -->
      <div class="col-lg-4 col-md-7 mb-30">
        <div class="tp-about-4-thumb mr-70 wow img-custom-anim-left" data-wow-duration="1.5s" data-wow-delay="0.2s">
          <!-- Replace with ECZ-related image -->
          <img class="w-100" src="assets/img/about/four/thumb.jpg" alt="Electoral Commission of Zambia">
        </div>
      </div>

      <!-- CONTENT -->
      <div class="col-lg-5 mb-30">
        <div class="tp-about-2-content tp-about-4-content">

          <span class="fw-500 fs-16 tp-text-grey-1 mb-5 d-inline-block">
            About the Commission
          </span>

          <h2 class="mb-20 fs-xl-40 fs-sm-36 wow img-custom-anim-right" data-wow-duration="1.5s" data-wow-delay="0.2s">
            Delivering <span class="text-italic fw-400 tp-ff-heading">credible, transparent and inclusive</span> elections.
          </h2>

          <p class="mb-30">
            The Electoral Commission of Zambia (ECZ) is mandated to manage and conduct elections in accordance with the
            Constitution and electoral laws. We serve citizens by providing voter services, administering polling and
            counting processes, and announcing official results with integrity and transparency.
          </p>

          <div class="tp-about-bottom-feature mb-40">
            <ul>
              <li><i class="fa-sharp fa-solid fa-check"></i> Voter services and civic information</li>
              <li><i class="fa-sharp fa-solid fa-check"></i> Polling operations and results management</li>
              <li><i class="fa-sharp fa-solid fa-check"></i> Stakeholder engagement and electoral transparency</li>
            </ul>
          </div>

          <a href="#services"
             class="tp-btn-xl d-inline-block lh-0 tp-round-26 fs-16 tp-bg-theme-primary ls-0 tp-btn-switch-animation tp-text-common-white hover-text-white fw-500">
            <span class="d-flex align-items-center justify-content-center">
              <span class="btn-text">Learn more about ECZ</span>
              <span class="btn-icon"><i class="fa-sharp fa-regular fa-arrow-right"></i></span>
              <span class="btn-icon"><i class="fa-sharp fa-regular fa-arrow-right"></i></span>
            </span>
          </a>

        </div>
      </div>

    </div>
  </div>
</div>

Sure — here’s a clean Affiliations / Partnerships section version of your block, with a heading and supporting paragraph added while keeping your existing structure and styling pattern:

HTML
<!-- Affiliations Section -->
<div class="tp-brands-area pt-100 pb-100" id="affiliations">
  <div class="container">

    <!-- Heading -->
    <div class="row justify-content-center mb-50">
      <div class="col-lg-8 text-center">
        <span class="fw-500 fs-16 tp-text-grey-1 mb-10 d-inline-block">
          Our Affiliations
        </span>

        <h2 class="mb-20 fs-xl-40 fs-sm-36">
          Trusted Partnerships <span class="text-italic fw-400 tp-ff-heading">that strengthen democracy</span>
        </h2>

        <p class="mb-0">
          The Electoral Commission of Zambia works closely with regional and international institutions,
          development partners, and electoral bodies to promote best practices, transparency, and continuous
          improvement in electoral management.
        </p>
      </div>
    </div>

    <!-- Logos -->
    <div class="row">
      <div class="col-lg-12">
        <div class="tp-brands-wrap">
          <ul>
            <li><a href="#"><img src="assets/img/brand/brands.png" alt="Partner Logo"></a></li>
            <li><a href="#"><img src="assets/img/brand/brands-2.png" alt="Partner Logo"></a></li>
            <li><a href="#"><img src="assets/img/brand/brands-3.png" alt="Partner Logo"></a></li>
            <li><a href="#"><img src="assets/img/brand/brands-4.png" alt="Partner Logo"></a></li>
            <li><a href="#"><img src="assets/img/brand/brands-5.png" alt="Partner Logo"></a></li>
          </ul>
        </div>
      </div>
    </div>

  </div>
</div>
